columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, open edX course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag} for MITxOnline and xPro courses, {org}/{course}/{run_tag} for edX.org courses

columns.name: openedx_user_id
columns.description: int, user ID on the corresponding open edX platform

columns.name: risk_probability
columns.description: float, probability scores between 0 and 1 representing the model-estimated probability that a student exhibits suspicious behavior. Higher values indicate a higher likelihood of suspicious behavior, while values closer to 0 indicate normal behavior.

columns.name: created_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when probability was calculated

columns.name: program_name
columns.description: str, name of the program. If program is split over multiple tracks, the program name is the same for all tracks. e.g. Data, Economics, and Design of Policy

columns.name: program_type
columns.description: str, type of the program, e.g. MicroMasters, Series, Professional Certificate, etc.

columns.name: total_enrollments
columns.description: int, count of course enrollments in the program, aggregated by user_email and courserun_readable_id

columns.name: unique_users
columns.description: int, count of unique users who took courses in the program, aggregated by user_email

columns.name: unique_countries
columns.description: int, count of unique countries of enrolled users, aggregated by user_email

columns.name: verified_enrollments
columns.description: int, count of verified (paid) enrollments in the program's courses, aggregated by user_email and courserun_readable_id

columns.name: unique_verified_users
columns.description: int, count of unique verified users enrolled in the program's courses, aggregated by user_email

columns.name: course_certificates
columns.description: int, count of course certificate earned for the courses in the program, aggregated by user_email and courserun_readable_id

columns.name: unique_course_certificate_earners
columns.description: int, count of unique users who earned a certificate for one of the program's courses, aggregated by user_email

columns.name: program_certificates
columns.description: int, count of program certificates earned for each program, aggregated by user_email. If a user gets the Statistics and Data Science program certificate in both the General and Social Sciences tracks, it would be counted as one program certificate.

schema_snapshot.description: MITx and xPro Program Summary report. A count of all users, enrollments (audit and verified), certificates and program credentials aggregated for each program.

columns.name: platform
columns.description: str, name of the platform

columns.name: user_email
columns.description: str, user email associated with their account on the corresponding platform

columns.name: full_name
columns.description: str, user first and last name associated with their account on the corresponding platform

columns.name: course_title
columns.description: str, title of the course.

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: string, unique string to identify a course run on the corresponding platform.

columns.name: section_title
columns.description: str, title of the section within the course

columns.name: subsection_title
columns.description: str, title of the subsection within the course

columns.name: unit_title
columns.description: str, title of the unit within the course

columns.name: num_of_page_views
columns.description: int, num of times the user viewed a page

columns.name: estimated_time_played
columns.description: int, seconds that a user played a video

columns.name: video_duration
columns.description: int, duration of the video in seconds

columns.name: problems_attempted
columns.description: int, count of problems attempted

columns.name: number_of_problems
columns.description: int, total number of problems regardless of whether they were attempted

columns.name: num_of_problems_correct
columns.description: int, count of distinct problems the user got correct

columns.name: avg_percent_grade
columns.description: str, average grade the learner recieved

columns.name: max_possible_grade
columns.description: str, max possible grade on a problem

columns.name: max_learner_grade
columns.description: str, highest learner grade on a problem

columns.name: min_learner_grade
columns.description: str, lowest learner grade on a problem

columns.name: posts_created
columns.description: int, count of discussion posts created

columns.name: posts_replied
columns.description: int, count of discussion posts replied to

columns.name: video_name
columns.description: str, name of the video within the course

columns.name: page_viewed_title
columns.description: str, title of the block viewed within the course

columns.name: problem_name
columns.description: str, name of the problem within the course

columns.name: discussion_topic_name
columns.description: str, name of the discussion topic within the course

schema_snapshot.description: Engagement report by learner, course, courserun, section and subsection. A count of posts replied, posts created, average percent grade, percent problems attempted, number of problems, problems attempted, video duration, estimated time played, and number of page views.

columns.name: platform
columns.description: string, application where the data is from

columns.name: courserunenrollment_id
columns.description: int, internal ID and foreign key to courserunenrollments on the corresponding platform. Null for enrollments on edX.org.

columns.name: course_readable_id
columns.description: str, Open edX ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code} for MITx Online, xPro or Residential courses, and {org}/{course} for edX.org courses. May be null for some Bootcamps courses. Examples include MITx/6.00.2x and course-v1:MITxT+14.740x.

columns.name: course_title
columns.description: str, title of the course. May be null for some edX.org and Residential courses

columns.name: courserun_is_current
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the course run is currently running. True if courserun_start_on is in the past and blank courserun_end_on, or courserun_start_on is in the past and courserun_end_on is in the future.

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, Open edX course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag} for MITxOnline and xPro courses, {org}/{course}/{run_tag} for edX.org courses

columns.name: courserun_start_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the course run starts. May be Null.

columns.name: courserun_end_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the course run ends. May be Null.

columns.name: courserun_title
columns.description: string, title of the course run on the corresponding platform. Maybe blank for a few edX.org runs missing in int__edxorg__mitx_courseruns.

columns.name: courserunenrollment_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, specifying when the enrollment was initially

columns.name: courserunenrollment_enrollment_mode
columns.description: string, enrollment mode for user on the corresponding platform

columns.name: courserunenrollment_enrollment_status
columns.description: string, enrollment status for users whose enrollment changed on the corresponding platform

columns.name: courserunenrollment_is_active
columns.description: boolean, indicating whether the enrollment is active

columns.name: courserunenrollment_upgraded_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the learner upgraded to the verified track course mode. May be the same as the date when the course enrollment was created for xPro.

columns.name: courseruncertificate_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the course certificate was initially created

columns.name: courseruncertificate_is_earned
columns.description: boolean, indicating if learner has earned the certificate on mitxonline.mit.edu, edX.org or xpro.mit.edu.

columns.name: courseruncertificate_url
columns.description: str, URL to the course certificate for users who earned the certificate on mitxonline.mit.edu, edX.org or xpro.mit.edu.

columns.name: courserungrade_grade
columns.description: float, course grade on edX.org or MITxOnline or xPro range from 0 to 1

columns.name: courserungrade_is_passing
columns.description: boolean, indicating whether the user has passed the passing score set for this course on edX.org or MITxOnline or xPro

columns.name: user_country_code
columns.description: str, country code on the platform user enrolled. May be blank.

columns.name: user_highest_education
columns.description: str, user's level of education

columns.name: user_full_name
columns.description: str, user full name on the platform user enrolled.

columns.name: user_username
columns.description: string, username to identify a user on the corresponding platform. Null for all the xPRO Emeritus.

columns.name: user_email
columns.description: string, user email that user registered on the corresponding platform. May be null for some xPRO Emeritus learners.

columns.name: num_of_course_passed
columns.description: number, the number of courses learner passed based on their emails so this counts as same person if they use the email to register on different platforms

columns.name: coupon_code
columns.description: string, discount code for the redeemed coupon if applicable

columns.name: coupon_name
columns.description: string, human readable name for the coupon payment

columns.name: discount
columns.description: str, discount applied to the order in a readable format. The value is always an amount taken off the list price of the order, e.g., "$100" means $100 was taken off the price. "30%" means the price was reduced by 30%.

columns.name: order_id
columns.description: int, foreign key to ecommerce order table for fulfilled or refunded orders on the corresponding platform. Note that this does not include orders from edX.org, for which we do not have data.

columns.name: order_reference_number
columns.description: string, order reference number to identify a fulfilled or refunded order on the corresponding platform, e.g. mitxonline-production-20

columns.name: order_state
columns.description: string, order state. Options are "cancelled", "created", "declined", "errored", "failed", "fulfilled", "refunded", "partially_refunded",  "pending", "review".

columns.name: order_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, specifying when the order was initially created. If this is a b2b order it will be the timestamp that order was created otherwise it will be when the regular order was created. For edX.org, it is the timestamp that order was created in MicroMasters application.

columns.name: receipt_payment_amount
columns.description: numeric, total amount for the order - req_amount from cybersource payment

columns.name: receipt_payment_method
columns.description: string, payment method from cybersource payment transaction. Value could be 'paypal' or 'card'.

columns.name: receipt_payment_timestamp
columns.description: string, the signed_date_time from cybersource payment transaction

columns.name: receipt_payer_email
columns.description: string, payer email address from cybersource payment transaction

columns.name: unit_price
columns.description: numeric, price for the order line item before discount

columns.name: program_name
columns.description: string, name of the program the course belongs to

columns.name: receipt_url
columns.description: string, URL to the receipt for the order on the MITx Online application

schema_snapshot.description: Enrollment detail report for MITxOnline, xPro and edX.org courses. A detailed view of enrollments, course runs, course certificates, orders, and user information for each enrollment.

columns.name: user_email
columns.description: str, user email on the corresponding platform

columns.name: chapter_title
columns.description: str, title of chapter this video belongs to. Null for Residential MITx until we populate the course structure data.

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, the open edX course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag} for MITxOnline, xPro, Residential courses, {org}/{course}/{run_tag} for edX.org courses.

columns.name: total_video_count
columns.description: int, the total number of distinct videos for a chapter and courserun.

columns.name: platform
columns.description: str, open edx platform, e.g., MITx Online, edX.org, xPro, Residential MITx.

columns.name: courserun_is_current
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the course run is currently running. True if courserun_start_on is in the past and blank courserun_end_on, or courserun_start_on is in the past and courserun_end_on is in the future.

columns.name: course_readable_id
columns.description: str, Open edX ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code} for MITx Online, xPro or Residential courses, {org}/{course} for edX.org courses.

columns.name: user_watched_video_count
columns.description: int, The number of distinct videos the user watched in the course

columns.name: max_video_index
columns.description: int, The maximum sequence number giving order in which this block content appears within the course.

schema_snapshot.description: Learners video engagement - play, pause, seek, or stop video actions from MITx Online, xPro, edX.org, Residential MITx with calculated counts for total video events, distinct videos watched, and max video index.

columns.name: platform
columns.description: str, open edx platform, e.g., MITx Online, edX.org, xPro, Residential MITx.

columns.name: openedx_user_id
columns.description: int, user ID on the corresponding open edX platform

columns.name: email
columns.description: str, user email

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: string, foreign key to dim_course_content, referencing the edX course run ID

columns.name: problem_block_fk
columns.description: string, foreign key referencing the problem block ID in the dim_course_content table, formatted as block-v1:{org}+{course}+{run}type@problem@{hash code}.

columns.name: unit_name
columns.description: string, title of the course content block. It is the name field for the unit on the Studio 'Course Outline' page.

columns.name: chapter_name
columns.description: string, title of the course content block. It is the name field for the chapter.

columns.name: sequential_name
columns.description: string, title of the course content block. It is the name field for the sequential.

columns.name: exam_indicator
columns.description: boolean, If the name field for the unit on the Studio 'Course Outline' page contains the word 'exam' then this is true otherwise it's false.

columns.name: hw_indicator
columns.description: boolean, If the name field for the unit on the Studio 'Course Outline' page contains the word 'homework' then this is true otherwise it's false.

columns.name: max_possible_grade
columns.description: number, maximum possible grade value for the problem

columns.name: attempts_on_problem
columns.description: int, max attempt number generated when parsing answer submissions

columns.name: max_learner_grade
columns.description: number, max of how many responses the learner got right within the problem.

columns.name: grades
columns.description: number, current values indicating the total unweighted grades for this problem that the learner has scored. e.g. how many responses they got right within the problem. This data is also available in courseactivity_problemcheck

columns.name: time_spent_on_problem
columns.description: string, the estimated amount of time spent on a problem. It cannot exceed 600 seconds or it will be null.

columns.name: time_spent_on_problem_nolimit
columns.description: string, the estimated amount of time spent on a problem.

columns.name: courserungrade_grade
columns.description: float, course grade on edX.org or MITxOnline or xPro range from 0 to 1

columns.name: user_avg_hw_grade
columns.description: number, avg of grades the learner got for all homework problems

columns.name: user_avg_exam_grade
columns.description: number, avg of grades the learner got for all exam problems

columns.name: user_hw_median_solving_time
columns.description: string, the estimated median amount of time the learner spent on all homework problems.

columns.name: user_exam_median_solving_time
columns.description: string, the estimated median amount of time the learner spent on all exam problems.

columns.name: user_hw_attempts_on_problem
columns.description: int, total learner attempts generated when parsing answer submissions on homework problems

columns.name: user_exam_time_flags
columns.description: int, total times a learner gets a performance time flag when their exam problem solving time was below the 10th percentile of time taken by all students to solve an exam problem

columns.name: user_non_exam_problem_count
columns.description: int, count of non-exam problems the learner attempted in the course

columns.name: user_taken_final_exam
columns.description: boolean, true if the learner has taken the final exam in the course

schema_snapshot.description: Problem and grade information that is associated with a particular course run, platform, and learner that is used for cheating detection analysis.

schema_snapshot.description: A report that contains student risk probability scores and course grade

columns.name: ai_agent
columns.description: str, indicates the type of chatbot agent used in the session. Possible values - TutorBot, CanvasSyllabusBot, ResourceRecommendationBot, VideoGPTBot, SyllabusBot

columns.description: str, the course readable ID for the chat session. May be blank for ResourceRecommendationBot

columns.name: thread_id
columns.description: str, unique identifier for the chat thread.

columns.name: resource_id
columns.description: str, course ID, video transcript asset ID, or edX module ID depending on the ai_agent. May be blank for ResourceRecommendationBot.

columns.name: human_message
columns.description: str, the human message

columns.name: agent_message
columns.description: str, the agent message

columns.name: message_index
columns.description: int, the order of the message in the chat session.

columns.name: created_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the chat session was created.

schema_snapshot.description: AI chatbot interactions from Learn AI application including TutorBot, ResourceRecommendationBot, SyllabusBot and VideoGPTBot. No personal information is included in this report.

columns.description: str, Open edX ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code} for MITx Online, xPro or Residential courses, and {org}/{course} for edX.org courses. May be null for some Bootcamps courses. Examples include MITx/6.00.2x and course-v1:MITxT+14.740x.

columns.name: course_title
columns.description: str, title of the course.

columns.name: courserun_id
columns.description: int, foreign key in courses_courserun. Null for edX.org.

columns.name: courserun_is_current
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the course run is currently running. True if courserun_start_on is in the past and blank courserun_end_on, or courserun_start_on is in the past and courserun_end_on is in the future.

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, Open edX course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag} for MITxOnline and xPro courses, {org}/{course}/{run_tag} for edX.org courses

columns.name: courserun_start_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the course run starts. May be Null.

columns.name: courserun_end_on
columns.description: timestamp, datetime on when the course ends

columns.name: courserun_title
columns.description: str, title of the course run

columns.name: courserun_upgrade_deadline
columns.description: timestamp, specifying the date time beyond which users can not enroll in paid course mode

columns.name: courseruncertificate_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the course certificate was initially created

columns.name: courseruncertificate_is_earned
columns.description: boolean, indicating if learner has earned the certificate on mitxonline.mit.edu, edX.org or xpro.mit.edu.

columns.name: courseruncertificate_url
columns.description: str, URL to the course certificate for users who earned the certificate on mitxonline.mit.edu, edX.org or xpro.mit.edu.

columns.name: courseruncertificate_uuid
columns.description: str, unique identifier for the certificate on the corresponding platform

columns.name: courserunenrollment_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, specifying when the enrollment was initially created

columns.name: courserunenrollment_enrollment_mode
columns.description: string, enrollment mode for user on the corresponding platform

columns.name: courserunenrollment_enrollment_status
columns.description: string, enrollment status for users whose enrollment changed on the corresponding platform

columns.name: courserunenrollment_is_active
columns.description: boolean, indicating whether the enrollment is active

columns.name: courserunenrollment_is_edx_enrolled
columns.description: boolean, indicating whether the user is enrolled on edX platform. For edx.org course enrollment, it would always be true. Null for Bootcamps as it doesn't apply.

columns.name: courserunenrollment_upgraded_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the learner upgraded to the verified track course mode. May be the same as the date when the course enrollment was created for xPro.

columns.name: courserungrade_grade
columns.description: float, course grade on edX.org or MITxOnline or xPro range from 0 to 1

columns.name: courserungrade_is_passing
columns.description: boolean, indicating whether the user has passed the passing score set for this course on edX.org or MITxOnline or xPro

columns.name: line_id
columns.description: int, foreign key to ecommerce line table for fulfilled or refunded orders on the corresponding platform. For edX.org, it refers to line table in MicroMasters application.

columns.name: order_id
columns.description: int, foreign key to ecommerce order table for fulfilled or refunded orders on the corresponding platform. Note that this does not include orders from edX.org, for which we do not have data.

columns.name: order_reference_number
columns.description: string, order reference number to identify a fulfilled or refunded order on the corresponding platform, e.g. mitxonline-production-20

columns.name: user_company

columns.name: user_country_code
columns.description: str, country code on the platform user enrolled. May be blank.

columns.description: string, user email that user registered on the corresponding platform. May be null for some xPRO Emeritus learners.

columns.name: user_full_name
columns.description: str, user full name on the platform user enrolled.

columns.name: user_highest_education
columns.description: str, user's highest education on the platform user enrolled. May be blank.

columns.description: str, foreign key to combined_users to identify the user

columns.description: int, user ID on the corresponding platform

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: string, the edX course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag}

columns.description: string, topic name

columns.name: category_name

columns.name: content_block_pk
columns.description: string, primary key for this table

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: string, the edX course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag} for courses on MITxOnline, xPro and Residential platform, {org}/{course}/{run_tag} for edX.org courses

columns.name: block_index
columns.description: int, sequence number giving order in which this block content appears within the course

columns.name: block_id
columns.description: string, a unique identifier for each content block within the course, formatted as block-v1:{org}+{course}+{run}type@{block type}+block@{hash code}

columns.name: parent_block_id
columns.description: string, parent content block ID, same format as content_block_id.

columns.name: chapter_block_id
columns.description: str, block id of chapter within which this child block belongs to. Null for course blocks.

columns.name: sequential_block_id
columns.description: str, block id of sequential/unit within which this child block belongs to. Null for course and chapter blocks.

columns.name: block_category
columns.description: string, category/type of the content block. Possible values are course, chapter, sequential, vertical, discussion, html, problem, video, etc.

columns.name: block_title
columns.description: string, title of the course content block. It is the name field for the section, subsection, or unit on the Studio 'Course Outline' page.

columns.name: block_metadata
columns.description: string, a JSON string representing the metadata field of the course content block. It provides additional information about this block, and different block types may have different member fields within the metadata.

columns.name: retrieved_at
columns.description: timestamp, date and time indicating when the content block was last retrieved from sources.

columns.name: is_latest
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the course content is the latest version

schema_snapshot.description: Slowly Changing Dimension model for course content, including courses, chapters, subsections, problems, videos, and other contents across the Open edX platforms.

columns.name: platform
columns.description: string, foreign key to dim_platform, referencing the platform where the navigation event occurred

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: string, foreign key to dim_course_content, referencing the edX course run ID

columns.name: event_type
columns.description: string, type of the navigation event, e.g. edx.ui.lms.jump_nav.selected, edx.ui.lms.link_clicked, edx.ui.lms.outline.selected, edx.ui.lms.sequence.next_selected, edx.ui.lms.sequence.previous_selected, edx.ui.lms.sequence.tab_selected, edx.ui.lms.video.played. The open edx doc - https://edx.readthedocs.io/projects/devdata/en/latest/internal_data_formats/tracking_logs/ student_event_types.html#course-navigation-events

columns.name: block_fk
columns.description: string, foreign key to dim_course_content, referencing the block ID of the page that is being navigated by the user. e.g. block-v1:{org}+{course}+{run}type@vertical+block@{hash code}.

columns.name: starting_position
columns.description: string, the starting position where the user is navigating from, either a page URL or the tab index in the unit navigation bar. Tabs correspond to the unit in the subsection (or verticals) and are indexed starting from 1. e.g. 1 for the first unit in the subsection.

columns.name: ending_position
columns.description: string, the ending position where the user is navigating to, either a page URL or the tab in the unit navigation bar. If ending_position is NULL but starting_position is not NULL, it means the user navigates from the first unit in one subsection to the last unit in the previous subsection using the "previous" control, or from the last unit in one subsection to the first unit in the next subsection using the "next" control.

columns.name: event_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, timestamp (with time zone) indicating the time at which the event occurred, formatted as timestamp(9)

columns.name: event_json
columns.description: json, it includes member fields that identify specifics of each triggered event. For less common/event-specific attributes.

schema_snapshot.description: Transactional fact table for learner navigation events in a course

columns.description: string, foreign key to dim_platform

columns.description: int, user ID on the corresponding open edX platform

columns.name: platform
columns.description: string, foreign key to dim_platform, referencing the platform where the video event occurred

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: string, foreign key to dim_course_content, referencing the edX course run ID

columns.name: event_type
columns.description: string, type of the video event. Possible values are load_video, pause_video, play_video, seek_video, speed_change_video, stop_video, and video_hide_transcript. The open edx doc - https://edx.readthedocs.io/projects/devdata/en/latest/internal_data_formats/ tracking_logs/student_event_types.html#video-interaction-events

columns.name: video_block_fk
columns.description: string, foreign key referencing a substring of the video block ID in the dim_course_content table. This value is the hash code of the video block, formatted as block-v1:{org}+{course}+{run}type@video@{hash code}.

columns.name: video_duration
columns.description: float, the length of the video, in seconds.

columns.name: video_position
columns.description: float, the seconds into the video where the event occurred. May be Null for `load_video` or `seek_video` events.

columns.name: starting_position
columns.description: float, the seconds into the video where the user was before seeking the video. For very small seek_video events, the `starting_position` may not be recorded in the residential dataset.

columns.name: ending_position
columns.description: float, the seconds into the video where the user was after seeking the video.

columns.name: event_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, timestamp (with time zone) indicating the time at which the event occurred, formatted as timestamp(9)

columns.name: event_json
columns.description: json, it includes member fields that identify specifics of each triggered event. For less common/event-specific attributes.

columns.description: json, JSON object indicating the user’s last known state in the course. It may contain any attributes for different module types - course, chapter, sequential, etc.

columns.name: answers

columns.name: attempt
columns.description: int, incremental attempt number generated when parsing answer submissions

columns.name: success

columns.name: grade
columns.description: number, current value indicating the total unweighted grade for this problem that the learner has scored. e.g. how many responses they got right within the problem. This data is also available in courseactivity_problemcheck

columns.name: max_grade

columns.description: timestamp, datetime when this row was last updated. A change in this field implies that there was a state change.

columns.description: str, username on Residential MITx

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, the edX user ID extracted from context field in tracking log. This id doesn't always match with the id in auth_user. There could be multiple user_ids for the same user_username. For those cases, use user_id from source table auth_user for that user_username.

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course}+{run} for course runs since Fall 2014, {org}/{course number}/{run} for older runs

columns.name: useractivity_event_source
columns.description: str, specifies the source of the interaction that triggered the event. The value is server for the discussion events.

columns.name: useractivity_event_type
columns.description: str, type of discussion forum event triggered. Values can be edx.forum.comment.created, edx.forum.comment.edited, edx.forum.response.created, edx.forum.response.voted, edx.forum.thread.created, edx.forum.thread.viewed, edx.forum.thread.voted, edx.forum.searched, etc. A list of discussion events can be found https://edx.readthedocs.io/projects/devdata/en/latest/ internal_data_formats/tracking_logs/student_event_types.html#discussion-forum-events

columns.name: useractivity_path
columns.description: str, relative url path of page that generated this discussion event. e.g. for edx.forum.response.voted events, it indicates if this event is unvote or upvote

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_post_id
columns.description: str, unique identifier for the discussion post learner engaged in. May be Null for edx.forum.searched event

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_post_title
columns.description: str, title for the specific discussion post. May be Null for edx.forum.searched event.

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_block_id
columns.description: str, identifier for the specific discussion component. e.g. discussion_ps2A-tab5. Value is the last part of 'coursestructure_block_id' string. May be Null for edx.forum.searched event.

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_block_name
columns.description: str, display name for the specific discussion component. This value consists of the unit name and 'coursestructure_block_title' of this discussion component.

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_page_url
columns.description: str, URL of the page from which the discussion thread can be viewed.

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_search_query
columns.description: str, the query text searched by the learner on the course discussion page. Applicable for edx.forum.searched event.

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_roles
columns.description: array, Identifies a user who doesn't have discussion management privileges as a 'Student', or a user who has discussion management privileges as a course 'Community TA', 'Moderator', or 'Administrator'. The value of role refers to name field in django_comment_client_role_users. https://edx.readthedocs.io/projects/devdata/en/latest/internal_data_formats/sql_schema.html#id12

columns.name: useractivity_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, time when this event was emitted

columns.description: str, username on Residential MITx

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, the edX user ID extracted from context field. This id doesn't always match with the id in auth_user. There could be multiple user_ids for the same user_username. For those cases, use user_id from source table auth_user for that user_username.

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course}+{run} for course runs since Fall 2014, {org}/{course number}/{run} for older runs

columns.name: useractivity_event_source
columns.description: str, specifies the source of the interaction that triggered the event. The value can only be browser for video event as MITx Online doesn't have a mobile app at this time.

columns.name: useractivity_event_type
columns.description: str, type of video event triggered. e.g. play_video, pause_video, stop_video, complete_video, etc. A list of video events can be found https://edx.readthedocs.io/projects/devdata/en/latest/ internal_data_formats/tracking_logs/student_event_types.html#video-interaction-events

columns.name: useractivity_event_object
columns.description: object, it includes member fields that identify the specific video event. Example, id, code, new_time, old_time, etc.

columns.name: useractivity_page_url
columns.description: str, url of the page the user was visiting when the event was emitted.

columns.name: useractivity_video_id
columns.description: str, hash code for the video being watched. This value is the last part of coursestructure_block_id string

columns.name: useractivity_video_duration
columns.description: number, The length of the video file, in seconds.

columns.name: useractivity_video_currenttime
columns.description: number, The time in the video when this event was emitted. May be Null for load_video or seek_video events.

columns.name: useractivity_video_old_time
columns.description: number, time in the video, in seconds, at which the user chose to go to a different point in time for seek_video event. Small seek_video events may not have old time recorded in this dataset.

columns.name: useractivity_video_new_time
columns.description: number, time in the video, in seconds, that the user selected as the destination point for seek_video event

columns.name: useractivity_video_new_speed
columns.description: number, new speed that the user selected for the video to play for speed_change_video event. e.g. 0.75, 1.0, 1.25, 1.50.

columns.name: useractivity_video_old_speed
columns.description: number, old speed at which the video was playing for speed_change_video event.

columns.name: useractivity_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, time when this event was emitted

columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag}

columns.name: coursestructure_content_hash
columns.description: str, sha256 hashed string of the course content.

columns.name: coursestructure_block_content_hash
columns.description: str, sha256 hashed string of the block content in a course

columns.name: coursestructure_block_id
columns.description: str, Unique ID for a distinct piece of content in a course, formatted as block-v1:{org}+{course}+{run}type@{block type}+block@{hash code}

columns.name: coursestructure_parent_block_id
columns.description: str, parent block ID, same format as block_id

columns.name: coursestructure_block_index
columns.description: int, sequence number giving order in which this block content appears within the course

columns.name: coursestructure_block_category
columns.description: str, category/type of the block, it identifies core structural elements of a course. Value includes but not limited to course, chapter, sequential, vertical, discussion, html, problem, video, etc.

columns.name: coursestructure_block_title
columns.description: str, title of the block extracted from the metadata of the block. This field comes from name field for the section, subsection, or unit on the Studio 'Course Outline' page.

columns.name: coursestructure_block_metadata
columns.description: str, json string of the metadata field for the block. It provides additional information about this block, different block type may have different member fields inside metadata.

columns.name: courserun_title
columns.description: str, title of the course extracted from the metadata of 'course' block

columns.name: courserun_start_on
columns.description: timestamp, indicating when the course run starts extracted from the metadata of 'course' block

columns.name: coursestructure_retrieved_at
columns.description: timestamp, indicating when this course structure was initially retrieved from REST API.

columns.name: coursestructure_chapter_id
columns.description: str, block id of chapter within which this child block belongs to. Null for the 'course' block as it's the top block that doesn't belong to any chapter.

columns.name: coursestructure_chapter_title
columns.description: str, title of chapter within which this child block belongs to.

columns.name: coursestructure_is_latest
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the course content is the latest version

schema_snapshot.description: This table contains the historical changes made to the course structure. It also includes a coursestructure_chapter_id that identifies the chapter to which each subsection belongs.

columns.description: string, transaction type, one of "refund" and "payment

columns.name: transaction_status
columns.description: str, transaction status from user's cybersource payment. Value could be ACCEPT, REVIEW. May be blank for no payment required or refund.

columns.name: transaction_payment_method
columns.description: str, payment method from cybersource Value could be 'paypal' or 'card'. May be blank for no payment required or refund.

columns.name: transaction_authorization_code
columns.description: str, authorization/approval code from cybersource payment or refund

columns.name: receipt_reference_number
columns.description: str, req_reference_number from cybersource payment

columns.name: transaction_bill_to_address_state
columns.description: str, address state from cybersource payment

columns.name: transaction_bill_to_address_country
columns.description: str, address country from cybersource payment

columns.name: transaction_uuid
columns.description: str, unique merchant-generated identifier - req_transaction_uuid from cybersource payment transaction, e.g., 02815b4f08e56882751a043839b7b481

columns.name: transaction_req_type
columns.description: str, type of transaction - req_transaction_type from cybersource payment transaction, e.g., sale

columns.name: transaction_payer_name
columns.description: str, payer name from cybersource payment transaction

columns.name: transaction_payer_email
columns.description: str, payer email from cybersource payment transaction

columns.name: transaction_payer_ip_address
columns.description: str, payer IP address from cybersource payment transaction

columns.name: transaction_payment_card_type
columns.description: str, name of the card type, card_type_name from cybersource payment transaction, e.g., Visa

columns.name: transaction_payment_card_number
columns.description: str, last 4 digits credit card number from cybersource payment transaction

columns.name: transaction_payment_amount
columns.description: numeric, total amount for the order - req_amount from cybersource payment transaction

columns.name: transaction_payment_currency
columns.description: str, currency used for the order - req_currency from cybersource payment transaction

columns.name: transaction_timestamp
columns.description: str, either the signed_date_time from cybersource payment transaction, or submitTimeUtc from cybersource refund transaction.

columns.name: order_id
columns.description: int, primary key representing a single MITx Online order.  Currently orders have exactly one line

columns.name: order_state
columns.description: string, order state. Options are "pending", "fulfilled", "canceled" "declined", "errored", "refunded", "review", "partially_refunded

columns.name: order_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, specifying when the order was initially created

columns.name: order_reference_number
columns.description: string, readable id for the order. Shared with micromasters

columns.name: order_total_price_paid
columns.description: number, total order amount

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, unique ID for each user on the MITx Online platform

columns.name: user_username
columns.description: string, username of purchaser

columns.name: user_full_name
columns.description: string, full name of purchaser

columns.name: user_email
columns.description: string, email of purchaser

columns.name: line_id
columns.description: int, primary key representing an ecommerce line

columns.name: product_version_id
columns.description: int. Versioned product

columns.name: product_price
columns.description: numeric, the price of the product for order line item

columns.name: product_type
columns.description: string, readable product type

columns.name: product_id
columns.description: int, sequential ID for ecommerce product

columns.name: courserun_id
columns.description: int, primary key representing a single MITx Online course run

columns.name: program_id
columns.description: int, primary key representing a single MITx Online program

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag}

columns.name: program_readable_id
columns.description: str, Open edX ID formatted as program-v1:{org}+{program code}

columns.name: discount_source
columns.description: str, type of discount coupon. Possible options are "marketing", "sales", "financial-assistance", "customer-support", "staff", "legacy

columns.name: discount_amount_text
columns.description: str, discount amount in readable format from the most recent coupon redemption for the order. It can be percent-off which is <dollar amount>% off, dollars-off which is $<dollar amount> off or fixed-price

columns.name: discount_amount
columns.description: numeric, actual discount dollar amount. For percent off discount, this is calculated as product price * percentage off

columns.name: discount_redemption_type
columns.description: str, one of  "one-time",  "one-time-per-user" and "unlimited

columns.name: discount_code
columns.description: str, discount code from the most recent coupon redemption for the order

columns.name: discountredemption_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, specifying when the discount was last redeemed by user for fulfilled or refunded orders

columns.name: transaction_id
columns.description: int, foreign key to ecommerce_transaction referring to cybersource payment

columns.name: payment_transaction_id
columns.description: str, unique identifier from most recent cybersource payment or UUID.

columns.name: payment_authorization_code
columns.description: str, authorization code from most recent cybersource payment

columns.name: payment_method
columns.description: str, payment method from most recent cybersource payment. Value could be 'paypal' or 'card'.

columns.name: payment_req_reference_number

columns.name: payment_bill_to_address_state
columns.description: str, address state from most recent cybersource payment

columns.name: payment_bill_to_address_country
columns.description: str, address country from most recent cybersource payment

schema_snapshot.description: Since mitxonline orders always have exactly one line, we can combine the ecommerce_line and ecommnerce_order tables into one intermediate table. This may change at some point in the future (xpro orders have multiple lines). If it does, the unique test on order_id in this model will fail and we will know to change this query and the queries that depend on it

columns.name: user_username
columns.description: str, username of the open edX user who caused the event to be emitted.

columns.name: openedx_user_id
columns.description: int, open edX user ID extracted from context field. This id doesn't always match with auth_user in open edX table, there can be multiple openedx_user_ids for the same user_username. For those cases, use openedx_user_id from auth_user open edX table for that user_username.

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag}

columns.name: useractivity_event_source
columns.description: str, specifies the source of the interaction that triggered the event. The value can only be browser for video event as MITx Online doesn't have a mobile app at this time.

columns.name: useractivity_event_type
columns.description: str, type of video event triggered. e.g. play_video, pause_video, stop_video, complete_video, etc. A list of video events can be found https://edx.readthedocs.io/projects/devdata/en/latest/ internal_data_formats/tracking_logs/student_event_types.html#video-interaction-events

columns.name: useractivity_page_url
columns.description: str, url of the page the user was visiting when the event was emitted.

columns.name: useractivity_video_id
columns.description: str, hash code for the video being watched. This value is the last part of coursestructure_block_id string

columns.name: useractivity_video_duration
columns.description: float, The length of the video file, in seconds. This is extracted from event field in tracking log.

columns.name: useractivity_video_currenttime
columns.description: number, The time in the video when this event was emitted. May be Null for load_video or seek_video events.

columns.name: useractivity_video_old_time
columns.description: number, time in the video, in seconds, at which the user chose to go to a different point in time for seek_video event

columns.name: useractivity_video_new_time
columns.description: number, time in the video, in seconds, that the user selected as the destination point for seek_video event

columns.name: useractivity_video_new_speed
columns.description: number, new speed that the user selected for the video to play for speed_change_video event. e.g. 0.75, 1.0, 1.25, 1.50.

columns.name: useractivity_video_old_speed
columns.description: number, old speed at which the video was playing for speed_change_video event.

columns.name: useractivity_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, time when this event was emitted

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag}

columns.name: useractivity_event_source
columns.description: str, specifies the source of the interaction that triggered the event. The value is server for the discussion events.

columns.name: useractivity_event_type
columns.description: str, type of discussion forum event triggered. Values can be edx.forum.comment.created, edx.forum.comment.edited, edx.forum.response.created, edx.forum.response.voted, edx.forum.thread.created, edx.forum.thread.viewed, edx.forum.thread.voted, edx.forum.searched, etc. A list of discussion events can be found https://edx.readthedocs.io/projects/devdata/en/latest/ internal_data_formats/tracking_logs/student_event_types.html#discussion-forum-events

columns.name: useractivity_path
columns.description: str, relative url path of page that generated this discussion event. e.g. for edx.forum.response.voted events, it indicates if this event is unvote or upvote

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_post_id
columns.description: str, unique identifier for the discussion post learner engaged in. May be Null for edx.forum.searched event

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_post_title
columns.description: str, title for the specific discussion post. May be Null for edx.forum.searched event.

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_block_id
columns.description: str, identifier for the specific discussion component. e.g. discussion_ps2A-tab5. Value is the last part of 'coursestructure_block_id' string. May be Null for edx.forum.searched event.

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_block_name
columns.description: str, display name for the specific discussion component. This value consists of the unit name and 'coursestructure_block_title' of this discussion component.

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_page_url
columns.description: str, URL of the page from which the discussion thread can be viewed.

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_search_query
columns.description: str, the query text searched by the learner on the course discussion page. Applicable for edx.forum.searched event.

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_roles
columns.description: array, Identifies a user who doesn't have discussion management privileges as a 'Student', or a user who has discussion management privileges as a course 'Community TA', 'Moderator', or 'Administrator'. The value of role refers to name field in django_comment_client_role_users. https://edx.readthedocs.io/projects/devdata/en/latest/internal_data_formats/sql_schema.html#id12

columns.name: useractivity_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, time when this event was emitted

columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag}

columns.name: coursestructure_content_hash
columns.description: str, sha256 hashed string of the course content.

columns.name: coursestructure_block_content_hash
columns.description: str, sha256 hashed string of the block content in a course

columns.name: coursestructure_block_id
columns.description: str, Unique ID for a distinct piece of content in a course, formatted as block-v1:{org}+{course}+{run}type@{block type}+block@{hash code}

columns.name: coursestructure_parent_block_id
columns.description: str, parent block ID, same format as block_id

columns.name: coursestructure_block_index
columns.description: int, sequence number giving order in which this block content appears within the course

columns.name: coursestructure_block_category
columns.description: str, category/type of the block, it identifies core structural elements of a course. Value includes but not limited to course, chapter, sequential, vertical, discussion, html, problem, video, etc.

columns.name: coursestructure_block_title
columns.description: str, title of the block extracted from the metadata of the block. This field comes from name field for the section, subsection, or unit on the Studio 'Course Outline' page.

columns.name: coursestructure_block_metadata
columns.description: str, json string of the metadata field for the block. It provides additional information about this block, different block type may have different member fields inside metadata.

columns.name: courserun_title
columns.description: str, title of the course extracted from the metadata of 'course' block

columns.name: courserun_start_on
columns.description: timestamp, indicating when the course run starts extracted from the metadata of 'course' block

columns.name: coursestructure_retrieved_at
columns.description: timestamp, indicating when this course structure was initially retrieved from REST API.

columns.name: coursestructure_chapter_id
columns.description: str, block id of chapter within which this child block belongs to. Null for the 'course' block as it's the top block that doesn't belong to any chapter.

columns.name: coursestructure_chapter_title
columns.description: str, title of chapter within which this child block belongs to.

columns.name: coursestructure_is_latest
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the course content is the latest version

schema_snapshot.description: this table contains historical changes to open edX's course content data. It adds coursestructure_chapter_id that identifies chapter each subsection belongs to

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag}

columns.name: coursestructure_block_id
columns.description: str, block ID of the subsection (sequential) - foreign key to course_structure

columns.name: visibleblocks_hash
columns.description: str, SHA1 hash of visible blocks within the subsection at the time of computing the subsection grade. This field might be useful for identifying whether the course content was the same for two grades.

columns.name: coursestructure_block_title
columns.description: str, title of the subsection

columns.name: coursestructure_chapter_id
columns.description: str, block id of chapter within which this subsection belongs to

columns.name: openedx_user_id

columns.name: subsectiongrade_total_score
columns.description: float, total score of all problems (both graded and ungraded) for the subsection

columns.name: subsectiongrade_total_earned_score
columns.description: float, user's total earned score of all problems (both graded and ungraded) for the subsection

columns.name: subsectiongrade_total_graded_score
columns.description: float, total score of all graded problems for the subsection.

columns.name: subsectiongrade_total_earned_graded_score
columns.description: float, user's total earned score of all graded problems for the subsection.

columns.name: subsectiongrade_is_overridden
columns.description: boolean, indicating whether this subsection grades are overridden

columns.name: subsectiongrade_first_attempted_on

columns.description: str, type of the checkpoint, e.g. msgpack

columns.name: checkpoint_namespace
columns.description: str, checkpoint namespace, used to group checkpoints in a thread.

columns.name: parent_checkpoint_id
columns.description: str, checkpoint ID of the parent checkpoint. Null if this is the first checkpoint in the thread.

columns.name: rating
columns.description: user rating for the chatbot response. E.g., like, dislike, '' for no rating.

columns.name: rating_reason
columns.description: user provided reason for the rating.

columns.name: checkpoint_created_on
columns.description: date and time when the checkpoint in the chat session was created.

columns.name: chatsession_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the chat session was created.

columns.name: chatsession_updated_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the chat session was last updated.

columns.name: platform
columns.description: str, indicating the platform where course runs on. It's either MITx Online or edX.org

columns.name: courserunenrollment_id
columns.description: int, internal ID and foreign key to courserunenrollments in MITx Online. Null for enrollments on edX.org.

columns.name: courserunenrollment_is_active
columns.description: boolean, indicating whether the user is still enrolled in the run

columns.name: courserunenrollment_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, specifying when an enrollment was initially created

columns.name: courserunenrollment_enrollment_mode
columns.description: str, enrollment mode for MITx courses. DEDP enrollments are verified in either MITx Online or MicroMasters orders. For DEDP runs on edX.org or DEDP runs on MITx Online in '3T2021', '1T2022', '2T2022', these are paid and verified in MicroMasters. For other DEDP courses that run on MITx Online, they are paid and verified in MITx Online

columns.name: courserunenrollment_enrollment_status
columns.description: str, enrollment status for users whose enrollment changed. Only applicable for MITx Online, In MITx Online options are 'deferred', 'transferred', 'refunded', 'enrolled', 'unenrolled', maybe blank

columns.name: courserunenrollment_is_edx_enrolled
columns.description: boolean, indicating whether the user is enrolled on edX platform. For edx.org course enrollment, it would always be true.

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, user ID on the corresponding platform - MITx Online or edX.org

columns.name: courserun_id
columns.description: int, primary key representing a single course run in MITx Online. Null for edX.org courses

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, course run ID on edX.org or MITxOnline

columns.name: courserun_title
columns.description: str, title of the course run, maybe blank for some edX.org runs

columns.name: courserun_start_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the course starts

columns.name: courserun_upgrade_deadline
columns.description: timestamp, specifying the date time beyond which users can not enroll in paid course mode

columns.name: user_email
columns.description: str, current user email on edX.org or MITxOnline. For edx.org, learners can update their emails, so this is not necessary the same email as when learner enrolled for the course

columns.name: user_username
columns.description: str, username on the corresponding platform - MITx Online or edX.org

columns.name: user_mitxonline_username
columns.description: str, username on MITx Online if user's account is linked on MicroMasters portal

columns.name: user_edxorg_username
columns.description: str, username in edx.org. For the very small number of users with multiple edx usernames, this is the username with the latest logins

columns.name: user_full_name
columns.description: str, user full name on edX.org or MITxOnline. Very small number of edX.org users have blank full name, their name couldn't be populated from other sources if they don't have their accounts linked on MicroMasters.

columns.name: course_number
columns.description: str, unique string for the course. It can contain letters, numbers, or periods. e.g. 6.002.1x

columns.name: user_address_country
columns.description: str, country code provided by the user from MITx Online or edX.org

columns.name: user_username
columns.description: str, username of the edX.org user

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, the edX user ID extracted from context field. This id doesn't always match with the id in auth_user. There could be multiple user_ids for the same user_username. For those cases, use user_id from source table auth_user for that user_username.

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, open edX Course ID formatted as {org}/{course number}/{run}

columns.name: useractivity_event_source
columns.description: str, specifies the source of the interaction that triggered the event. The value is server for the discussion events.

columns.name: useractivity_event_type
columns.description: str, type of discussion forum event triggered. Values can be edx.forum.comment.created, edx.forum.comment.edited, edx.forum.response.created, edx.forum.response.voted, edx.forum.thread.created, edx.forum.thread.viewed, edx.forum.thread.voted, edx.forum.searched, etc. A list of discussion events can be found https://edx.readthedocs.io/projects/devdata/en/latest/ internal_data_formats/tracking_logs/student_event_types.html#discussion-forum-events

columns.name: useractivity_path
columns.description: str, relative url path of page that generated this discussion event. e.g. for edx.forum.response.voted events, it indicates if this event is unvote or upvote

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_post_id
columns.description: str, unique identifier for the discussion post learner engaged in. May be Null for edx.forum.searched event

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_post_title
columns.description: str, title for the specific discussion post. May be Null for edx.forum.searched event.

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_block_id
columns.description: str, identifier for the specific discussion component. e.g. discussion_ps2A-tab5. Value is the last part of 'coursestructure_block_id' string. May be Null for edx.forum.searched event.

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_block_name
columns.description: str, display name for the specific discussion component. This value consists of the unit name and 'coursestructure_block_title' of this discussion component.

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_page_url
columns.description: str, URL of the page from which the discussion thread can be viewed.

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_search_query
columns.description: str, the query text searched by the learner on the course discussion page. Applicable for edx.forum.searched event.

columns.name: useractivity_discussion_roles
columns.description: array, Identifies a user who doesn't have discussion management privileges as a 'Student', or a user who has discussion management privileges as a course 'Community TA', 'Moderator', or 'Administrator'. The value of role refers to name field in django_comment_client_role_users. https://edx.readthedocs.io/projects/devdata/en/latest/internal_data_formats/sql_schema.html#id12

columns.name: useractivity_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, time when this event was emitted

schema_snapshot.description: users' discussion forum activities within a course on edX.org

columns.description: str, username of the edX.org user

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, the edX user ID extracted from context field. This id doesn't always match with the id in auth_user. There could be multiple user_ids for the same user_username. For those cases, use user_id from source table auth_user for that user_username.

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, open edX Course ID formatted as {org}/{course number}/{run}

columns.name: useractivity_event_source
columns.description: str, specifies the source of the interaction that triggered the event. The value can only be browser for video event as MITx Online doesn't have a mobile app at this time.

columns.name: useractivity_event_type
columns.description: str, type of video event triggered. e.g. play_video, pause_video, stop_video, complete_video, etc. A list of video events can be found https://edx.readthedocs.io/projects/devdata/en/latest/ internal_data_formats/tracking_logs/student_event_types.html#video-interaction-events

columns.name: useractivity_page_url
columns.description: str, url of the page the user was visiting when the event was emitted.

columns.name: useractivity_video_id
columns.description: str, hash code for the video being watched. This value is the last part of coursestructure_block_id string

columns.name: useractivity_video_duration
columns.description: float, The length of the video file, in seconds.

columns.name: useractivity_video_currenttime
columns.description: number, The time in the video when this event was emitted. May be Null for load_video or seek_video events.

columns.name: useractivity_video_old_time
columns.description: number, time in the video, in seconds, at which the user chose to go to a different point in time for seek_video event

columns.name: useractivity_video_new_time
columns.description: number, time in the video, in seconds, that the user selected as the destination point for seek_video event

columns.name: useractivity_video_new_speed
columns.description: number, new speed that the user selected for the video to play for speed_change_video event. e.g. 0.75, 1.0, 1.25, 1.50.

columns.name: useractivity_video_old_speed
columns.description: number, old speed at which the video was playing for speed_change_video event.

columns.name: useractivity_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, time when this event was emitted

columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID formatted as {org}/{course code}/{run_tag}.

columns.name: coursestructure_content_hash
columns.description: str, sha256 hashed string of the course content.

columns.name: coursestructure_block_content_hash
columns.description: str, sha256 hashed string of the block content in a course

columns.name: coursestructure_block_id
columns.description: str, Unique ID for a distinct piece of content in a course, formatted as block-v1:{org}+{course}+{run}type@{block type}+block@{hash code}

columns.name: coursestructure_parent_block_id
columns.description: str, parent block ID, same format as block_id

columns.name: coursestructure_block_index
columns.description: int, sequence number giving order in which this block content appears within the course

columns.name: coursestructure_block_category
columns.description: str, category/type of the block, it identifies core structural elements of a course. Value includes but not limited to course, chapter, sequential, vertical, discussion, html, problem, video, etc.

columns.name: coursestructure_block_title
columns.description: str, title of the block extracted from the metadata of the block. This field comes from name field for the section, subsection, or unit on the Studio 'Course Outline' page.

columns.name: coursestructure_block_metadata
columns.description: str, json string of the metadata field for the block. It provides additional information about this block, different block type may have different member fields inside metadata.

columns.name: courserun_title
columns.description: str, title of the course extracted from the metadata of 'course' block

columns.name: coursestructure_retrieved_at
columns.description: timestamp, indicating when this course structure was initially retrieved from S3

columns.name: coursestructure_chapter_id
columns.description: str, block id of chapter within which this child block belongs to. Null for the 'course' block as it's the top block that doesn't belong to any chapter.

columns.name: coursestructure_chapter_title
columns.description: str, title of chapter within which this child block belongs to.

columns.name: coursestructure_is_latest
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the course content is the latest version

schema_snapshot.description: this table contains historical changes to edX.org course content data. It adds coursestructure_chapter_id that identifies chapter each subsection belongs to

columns.name: user_username
columns.description: str, username of a learner on edX.org

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, learner's user ID on edX.org. Extracted from context field.

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, open edX Course ID formatted as {org}/{course number}/{run}.

columns.name: useractivity_path
columns.description: str, URL that generated this event. Extracted from context field

columns.name: useractivity_context_object
columns.description: object, it includes member fields that provide contextual information. Common fields apply to all events are course_id, org_id, path, user_id. Other member fields for applicable events are course_user_tags, module.

columns.name: useractivity_event_source
columns.description: str, specifies the source of the interaction that triggered the event. The values are - browser, mobile, server, task

columns.name: useractivity_event_type
columns.description: str, type of event triggered. Values depend on event_source.

columns.name: useractivity_event_object
columns.description: object,it includes member fields that identify specifics of each triggered event. Different member fields are supplied for different events.

columns.name: useractivity_event_name
columns.description: str, type of event triggered. When this field is present for an event, it supersedes the event_type field.

columns.name: useractivity_page_url
columns.description: str, url of the page the user was visiting when the event was emitted.

columns.name: useractivity_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, time at which the event was emitted, formatted as ISO 8601 string

columns.description: string, one of "staff", "marketing", "credit_card", "purchase_order", "sales". "staff" coupons are used to allow staff to enroll in courses. "marketing" coupons are promos for discounts generated by marketing. "credit_card", "purchase_order" and "sales" correspond to different ways that companies can pay for vouchers for their employees to enroll in courses or programs. "b2bcoupon" coupons are sourced from the int__mitxpro__b2becommerce_b2bcoupon table and represent a coupon used by a business which can include many individuals.

schema_snapshot.description: The ecommerce_couponpaymentversion holds information about a batch of coupons. A coupon payment version has information about the dollar or percentage amount of the discount, the number of available coupons that can be used to redeem the discount, how many times they can be redeemed and the source of the coupons. Coupon groups can be generated internally, for example to allow staff to enroll in courses without paying or marketing to create promos that allow learners to take a percentage or dollar amount off the full course price. Other coupon groups are created when a company buys bulk seats in a course for their employees, which generates a batch of 100% off coupons that learners can use to enroll in a course or program.

columns.name: receipt_payment_timestamp

columns.description: int, foreign key to ecommerce_couponpaymentversion for orders that use a coupon

columns.name: couponpaymentversion_coupon_type
columns.description: string, one of 'single-use' or 'promo'. Promo coupon codes can be used multiple times

columns.name: couponpaymentversion_discount_source
columns.description: string, one of "staff", "marketing", "credit_card", "purchase_order", "sales". "staff" coupons are used to allow staff to enroll in courses. "marketing" coupons are promos for discounts generated by marketing. "credit_card", "purchase_order" and "sales" correspond to different ways that companies can pay for vouchers for their employees to enroll in courses or programs.

columns.name: couponpaymentversion_discount_type
columns.description: one of percent-off or dollars-off

columns.name: couponpaymentversion_discount_amount
columns.description: number, either dollar amount or percentage (between 0 and 1) discount

columns.name: couponpaymentversion_discount_amount_text
columns.description: string, discount amount in readable format. It can be percent-off which is <dollar amount * 100>% off, dollars-off which is $<dollar amount> off

columns.name: couponredemption_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, specifying when the coupon was redeemed by user for fulfilled or refunded orders

columns.description: str, payment method from cybersource payment. Value could be 'paypal' or 'card' if payment is not cancelled

columns.name: receipt_authorization_code

columns.name: receipt_bill_to_address_state
columns.description: str, address state from cybersource payment

columns.name: receipt_bill_to_address_country
columns.description: str, address country from cybersource payment

columns.description: int, open edX user ID extracted from context field. This id doesn't always match with auth_user in open edX table, there can be multiple openedx_user_ids for the same user_username. For those cases, use openedx_user_id from auth_user open edX table for that user_username.

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag}

columns.name: useractivity_event_source
columns.description: str, specifies the source of the interaction that triggered the event. The values are either browser or mobile for this event

columns.name: useractivity_event_type
columns.description: str, type of video event triggered. e.g. play_video, pause_video, stop_video, complete_video, etc. A list of video events can be found https://edx.readthedocs.io/projects/devdata/en/latest/ internal_data_formats/tracking_logs/student_event_types.html#video-interaction-events

columns.name: useractivity_page_url
columns.description: str, url of the page the user was visiting when the event was emitted.

columns.name: useractivity_video_id
columns.description: str, hash code for the video being watched. This value is the last part of coursestructure_block_id string

columns.name: useractivity_video_duration
columns.description: float, The length of the video file, in seconds. This is extracted from event field in tracking log.

columns.name: useractivity_video_currenttime
columns.description: number, The time in the video when this event was emitted

columns.name: useractivity_video_old_time
columns.description: number, time in the video, in seconds, at which the user chose to go to a different point in time for seek_video event

columns.name: useractivity_video_new_time
columns.description: number, time in the video, in seconds, that the user selected as the destination point for seek_video event

columns.name: useractivity_video_new_speed
columns.description: number, new speed that the user selected for the video to play for speed_change_video event. e.g. 0.75, 1.0, 1.25, 1.50.

columns.name: useractivity_video_old_speed
columns.description: number, old speed at which the video was playing for speed_change_video event.

columns.name: useractivity_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, time when this event was emitted

schema_snapshot.description: It tracks xPro open edX users video activities within a course

columns.name: user_username
columns.description: str, username of the open edX user who caused the event to be emitted.

columns.name: openedx_user_id
columns.description: int, reference user id in auth_user from open edX.

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag}

columns.name: useractivity_event_type
columns.description: str, problem_check - when a problem is successfully checked.

columns.name: useractivity_problem_id
columns.description: str, Unique ID for this problem in a course. It's recorded as a URL format - block-v1:{org)+{course ID}+type@problem+block@{hash code}

columns.name: useractivity_problem_name
columns.description: str, display name of this problem in a course

columns.name: useractivity_problem_student_answers
columns.description: json, student answers to this problem in problem_id and internal answer pair. For multiple questions, it lists every question and answer.

columns.name: useractivity_problem_attempts
columns.description: number, The number of times the user attempted to answer this problem

columns.name: useractivity_problem_success
columns.description: str, It's either 'correct' or 'incorrect

columns.name: useractivity_problem_current_grade
columns.description: number, current grade value for this user

columns.name: useractivity_problem_max_grade
columns.description: number, Maximum possible grade value for this problem

columns.name: useractivity_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, time when this event was emitted

schema_snapshot.description: It tracks xPro open edX users problem_check activities within a course. Some events from 2019 don't have nanosecond in their timestamp, if learners had problem check events during those periods, use problem_attempts, problem_success or other fields to gauge which one happens first
