columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the learner first passed the course. If this value is empty, the learner never passed the course. If this value is non-empty but the letter_grade value is empty, the learner transitioned from passing to not passing.

columns.name: courserungrade_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when this grade was initially created

columns.name: courserungrade_updated_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when this grade was most recently updated

schema_snapshot.description: Residential MITx open edX course grades

columns.name: user_username
columns.description: str, username on Residential MITx

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, the edX user ID extracted from context field in tracking log

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course}+{run}. Extracted from various fields - context.course_id, context.path, event_type and page. Note that the course ID extracted from context field may not be a valid. This field could be blank for any events that are not for any specific course .e.g. user login/out.

columns.name: org_id
columns.description: str, reference name in organizations_organization from open edX. e.g. MITx . Extracted from context field.

columns.name: useractivity_path
columns.description: str, URL that generated this event. Extracted from context field

columns.name: useractivity_context_object
columns.description: object, it includes member fields that provide contextual information. Common fields apply to all events are course_id, org_id, path, user_id. Other member fields for applicable events are course_user_tags, module.

columns.name: useractivity_event_source
columns.description: str, specifies the source of the interaction that triggered the event. The possible values are - browser, server, task.

columns.name: useractivity_event_type
columns.description: str, type of event triggered. Values depend on event_source.

columns.name: useractivity_event_object
columns.description: object,it includes member fields that identify specifics of each triggered event. Different member fields are supplied for different events.

columns.name: useractivity_page_url
columns.description: str, url of the page the user was visiting when the event was emitted.

columns.name: useractivity_session_id
columns.description: str, 32-character value to identify the user’s session. All browser events and the server 'enrollment' events include session value. Other server events do not include a session value.

columns.name: useractivity_ip
columns.description: str, IP address of the user who triggered the event.

columns.name: useractivity_http_host
columns.description: str, The site visited by the user, e.g., lms.mitx.mit.edu

columns.name: useractivity_http_user_agent
columns.description: str, browser agent string of the user who triggered the event.

columns.name: useractivity_http_accept_language
columns.description: str, value from the HTTP Accept-Language request-header field

columns.name: useractivity_http_referer
columns.description: str, URI from the HTTP Referer request-header field

columns.name: useractivity_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, time at which the event was emitted, formatted as ISO 8601 string

schema_snapshot.description: Residential MITx event data that are emitted by server and the browser to capture information about user's interactions with a course. This table filters out events that don't supply user identifiers like username and user_id.

columns.name: studentmodule_problem_max_grade
columns.description: float, floating point value indicating the total possible unweighted grade for this problem, or basically the number of responses that are in this problem. This data is also available in courseactivity_problemcheck

columns.name: studentmodule_created_on

columns.name: studentmodule_updated_on

schema_snapshot.description: It holds the most current course state and score per course section for learners on Residential MITx . There is a separate row for every piece of content that a learner accesses.

columns.name: coursestructure_retrieved_at

schema_snapshot.description: This table captures the historical changes made to the Residential MITx course structure by comparing the new course block to the course block from the previous day. If no changes are detected, no new rows are added to the table. If there are any changes to the course structure, all blocks, including the updated or new ones, are added to the table.

columns.description: str, different types of nodes that exist in a requirement tree - program_root (represent a program) - course (represent a reference to a course) - program (represent a reference to a program) - operator (represent a logical operation over a set of courses)

columns.name: programrequirement_operator_value
columns.description: int, value of the operator for this requirement e.g. if operator is all_of, the corresponding operator_value 3 means all of 3 courses

columns.name: user_username
columns.description: str, username of the open edX user who caused the event to be emitted.

columns.name: openedx_user_id
columns.description: int, open edX user ID extracted from context field. This id doesn't always match with auth_user in open edX table, there can be multiple openedx_user_ids for the same user_username. For those cases, use openedx_user_id from auth_user open edX table.

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course number}+{run_tag}. Extracted from various fields - context.course_id, context.path, event_type and page. The course ID extracted from context field may not be valid, it would require joining with courserun table to be sure. This field could be blank for any events that are not for any specific course .e.g. user login/out, visiting dashboard, some course team events e.g. course export from studio

columns.name: org_id
columns.description: str, reference name in organizations_organization from open edX. e.g. MITxT . Extracted from context field

columns.name: useractivity_path
columns.description: str, URL that generated this event. Extracted from context field

columns.name: useractivity_context_object
columns.description: object, it includes member fields that provide contextual information. Common fields apply to all events are course_id, org_id, path, user_id. Other member fields for applicable events are course_user_tags, module.

columns.name: useractivity_event_source
columns.description: str, specifies the source of the interaction that triggered the event. The values are - browser, mobile, server, task

columns.name: useractivity_event_type
columns.description: str, type of event triggered. Values depend on event_source.

columns.name: useractivity_event_name
columns.description: str, type of event triggered. When this field is present for an event, it supersedes the event_type field.

columns.name: useractivity_event_object
columns.description: object,it includes member fields that identify specifics of each triggered event. Different member fields are supplied for different events.

columns.name: useractivity_page_url
columns.description: str, url of the page the user was visiting when the event was emitted.

columns.name: useractivity_session_id
columns.description: str, 32-character value to identify the user’s session. All browser events and the server 'enrollment' events include session value. Other server events and mobile events do not include a session value.

columns.name: useractivity_ip
columns.description: str, IP address of the user who triggered the event. Empty for mobile events.

columns.name: useractivity_http_host
columns.description: str, The site visited by the user. e.g. courses.mitxonline.mit.edu

columns.name: useractivity_http_user_agent
columns.description: str, browser agent string of the user who triggered the event.

columns.name: useractivity_http_accept_language
columns.description: str, value from the HTTP Accept-Language request-header field

columns.name: useractivity_http_referer
columns.description: str, URI from the HTTP Referer request-header field

columns.name: useractivity_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, time at which the event was emitted, formatted as ISO 8601 string

schema_snapshot.description: This table is cleaned to remove duplicate events, events without user identifiers , and events that represent server errors

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag}

columns.name: coursestructure_content_hash
columns.description: str, sha256 hashed string of the course content.

columns.name: coursestructure_block_content_hash
columns.description: str, sha256 hashed string of the block content in a course

columns.name: coursestructure_block_id
columns.description: str, Unique ID for a distinct piece of content in a course, formatted as block-v1:{org}+{course}+{run}type@{block type}+block@{hash code}

columns.name: coursestructure_parent_block_id
columns.description: str, parent block ID, same format as block_id

columns.name: coursestructure_block_index
columns.description: int, sequence number giving order in which this block content appears within the course

columns.name: coursestructure_block_category
columns.description: str, category/type of the block, it identifies core structural elements of a course. Value includes but not limited to course, chapter, sequential, vertical, discussion, html, problem, video, etc.

columns.name: coursestructure_block_title
columns.description: str, title of the block extracted from the metadata of the block. This field comes from name field for the section, subsection, or unit on the Studio 'Course Outline' page.

columns.name: coursestructure_block_metadata
columns.description: str, json string of the metadata field for the block. It provides additional information about this block, different block type may have different member fields inside metadata.

columns.name: courserun_title
columns.description: str, title of the course extracted from the metadata of 'course' block

columns.name: courserun_start_on
columns.description: timestamp, indicating when the course run starts extracted from the metadata of 'course' block

columns.name: coursestructure_retrieved_at
columns.description: timestamp, indicating when this course structure was initially retrieved from REST API.

schema_snapshot.description: This table captures the historical changes made to the MITx Online course structure by comparing the new course block to the course block from the previous day. If no changes are detected, no new rows are added to the table. If there are any changes to the course structure, all blocks, including the updated or new ones, are added to the table.

columns.name: studentmodule_id
columns.description: int, primary key for this table

columns.name: openedx_user_id
columns.description: int, reference user id in auth_user from open edX

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID in the format course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag}

columns.name: coursestructure_block_id
columns.description: str, block ID for a distinct piece of content in a course, referencing course_structure

columns.name: coursestructure_block_category
columns.description: str, category/type of the block, referencing course_structure.

columns.name: studentmodule_state_data
columns.description: json, JSON object indicating the user’s last known state in the course. It may contain any attributes for different module types - course, chapter, sequential, etc.

columns.name: studentmodule_problem_grade
columns.description: float, floating point value indicating the total unweighted grade for this problem that the learner has scored. e.g. how many responses they got right within the problem. This data is also available in courseactivity_problemcheck

columns.name: studentmodule_problem_max_grade
columns.description: float, floating point value indicating the total possible unweighted grade for this problem, or basically the number of responses that are in this problem. This data is also available in courseactivity_problemcheck

columns.name: studentmodule_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, datetime when this row was created, which is typically when the learner first accesses this piece of content.

columns.name: studentmodule_updated_on
columns.description: timestamp, datetime when this row was last updated. A change in this field implies that there was a state change.

schema_snapshot.description: It holds the most current course state and score per course section for learners on MITx Online. There is a separate row for every piece of content that a learner accesses.

columns.name: subsectiongrade_id
columns.description: int, primary key in grades_subsectiongrade

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag}

columns.name: coursestructure_block_id
columns.description: str, block ID of the subsection (sequential) - foreign key to course_structure. e.g. block-v1:{org}+{course}+{run}type@{block type}+block@{hash code}

columns.name: openedx_user_id
columns.description: int, foreign key to auth_user from open edX platform

columns.name: visibleblocks_hash
columns.description: str, SHA1 hash of visible blocks within the subsection - foreign key to grades_visibleblocks. This field might be useful for identifying whether the course content was the same for two grades.

columns.name: subsectiongrade_total_score
columns.description: float, the aggregated "total_weighted_possible" score in the subsection, calculated by summing all "weighted_possible" values of all (graded and ungraded) problems in the subsection.

columns.name: subsectiongrade_total_graded_score
columns.description: float, The aggregated "total_weighted_possible" score in the subsection, calculated by summing all "weighted_possible" values of all "graded" problems in the subsection.

columns.name: subsectiongrade_total_earned_score
columns.description: float, The user's aggregated "total_weighted_earned" score in the subsection, calculated by summing all "weighted_earned" values of all (graded and ungraded) problems in the subsection.

columns.name: subsectiongrade_total_earned_graded_score
columns.description: float, The user's aggregated "total_weighted_earned" score in the subsection, calculated by summing all "weighted_earned" values of all "graded" problems in the subsection.

columns.name: subsectiongrade_first_attempted_on
columns.description: timestamp, time of the user's first attempt at a problem in the subsection.

columns.name: subsectiongrade_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, datetime of the subsection grade was first calculated for this user for this subsection.

columns.name: subsectiongrade_updated_on
columns.description: timestamp, datetime of the subsection grade was last updated for this user for this subsection.It will be updated every time the user attempts the problem.

schema_snapshot.description: Persistent values for learners' subsection (aka sequential) grades.

columns.name: subsectiongradeoverride_id

columns.name: subsectiongradeoverride_total_earned_score

columns.name: id
columns.description: int, primary key representing a MITx Online program requirement node

columns.name: course_id
columns.description: int, foreign key to courses_course representing a single course

columns.name: program_id
columns.description: int, foreign key to courses_program representing a single program

columns.name: node_type
columns.description: str, there are 3 types of nodes that exist in a requirement tree - program_root (represent a program) - course (represent a reference to a course) - operator (represent a logical operation over a set of courses)

columns.name: path
columns.description: str, the full materialized path for each node (readonly) e.g. 0001 is root path, 00010001 is the path to a node in this requirement tree

columns.name: depth
columns.description: int, depth of a node in the tree. A root node has a depth of 1. (readonly)

columns.name: numchild
columns.description: int, the number of children of a node (readonly)

columns.name: operator
columns.description: str, the logical operation over a set of courses, it can be all_of, min_number_of or blank

columns.name: title
columns.description: str, title of the operator for this requirement

columns.name: operator_value
columns.description: str, value of the operator for this requirement e.g. if operator is all_of, the corresponding operator_value 3 means all of 3 courses

columns.name: id
columns.description: int, sequential ID representing a user on MITx Online open edX platform

columns.name: username
columns.description: str, unique username on the open edX platform

columns.name: password
columns.description: str, hashed password

columns.name: first_name
columns.description: str, user's first name. This field is not populated on production.

columns.name: last_name
columns.description: str, user's last name. This field is not populated on production.

columns.name: email
columns.description: str, user's email associated with their account

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: boolean, used to soft delete users

columns.name: is_staff
columns.description: boolean, indicating whether user can log into admin site

columns.name: is_superuser
columns.description: boolean, indicating whether user has all permissions on open edx platform

columns.name: date_joined
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when a user account was initially created on the open edX platform

columns.name: last_login
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when user last login on the open edX platform

columns.name: username
columns.description: str, username of the open edX user who caused the event to be emitted. Some events are recorded with a blank username. This can occur when a user logs out, or the login session times out, while a browser window remains open. EdX recommends to ignore these events.

columns.name: context
columns.description: object, it includes member fields that provide contextual information. Common fields apply to all events are course_id, org_id, path, user_id. Other member fields for applicable events are course_user_tags, module.

columns.name: event_source
columns.description: str, specifies the source of the interaction that triggered the event. The values are - browser, mobile, server, task

columns.name: event_type
columns.description: str, type of event triggered. Values depend on event_source.

columns.name: name
columns.description: str, type of event triggered. When this field is present for an event, it supersedes the event_type field.

columns.name: event
columns.description: object, it includes member fields that identify specifics of each triggered event. Different member fields are supplied for different events.

columns.name: page
columns.description: str, url of the page the user was visiting when the event was emitted.

columns.name: session
columns.description: str, 32-character value to identify the user’s session. All browser events and the server 'enrollment' events include a session value. Other server events and mobile events do not include a session value.

columns.name: ip
columns.description: str, IP address of the user who triggered the event. Empty for mobile events.

columns.name: host
columns.description: str, the site visited by the user. e.g. courses.mitxonline.mit.edu

columns.name: time
columns.description: str, time at which the event was emitted. It has inconsistent formats due to log collector switches, YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss.SSSSSS for older records, YYYY-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSSSS for newer records

columns.name: agent
columns.description: str, browser agent string of the user who triggered the event.

columns.name: accept_language
columns.description: str, value from the HTTP Accept-Language request-header field

columns.name: referer
columns.description: str, URI from the HTTP Referer request-header field

columns.name: log_file
columns.description: str, internal used field for log file location

columns.name: _ab_source_file_url
columns.description: str, url path for the raw log file

columns.name: vector_timestamp
columns.description: str, time when Vector processed this record. Blank for old records.

columns.name: environment
columns.description: str, internal used field to indicate environment for the event. e.g. mitxonline-production

columns.name: course_id
columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag}

columns.name: content_hash
columns.description: str, sha256 hashed string for the course content

columns.name: course_structure
columns.description: str, json string of the course content

columns.name: course_structure_flattened
columns.description: str, flatten json string of the course content. It flattens block's category, metadata and children to same level.

columns.name: retrieved_at
columns.description: timestamp, time indicating when this course structure was initially retrieved from REST API.

columns.name: block_id
columns.description: str, Unique ID for a distinct piece of content in a course, formatted as block-v1:{org}+{course}+{run}type@{block type}+block@{hash code}

columns.name: block_index
columns.description: int, sequence number giving order in which this block content appears within the course

columns.name: block_parent
columns.description: str, parent block ID, same format as block_id field

columns.name: block_type
columns.description: str, category/type of the block, it identifies core structural elements of a course. Value includes but not limited to course, chapter, sequential, vertical, discussion, html, problem, video, etc.

columns.name: block_title
columns.description: str, title of the block extracted from the metadata field of the block. This field comes from name field for the section, subsection, or unit on the Studio 'Course Outline' page

columns.name: block_details
columns.description: str, json string of the block. It contains the attribute fields like category, children and metadata. Different block type may have different member fields inside metadata.

columns.name: course_id
columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag}

columns.name: course_title
columns.description: str, title of the course extracted from the metadata field of 'course' block

columns.name: course_content_hash
columns.description: str, sha256 hashed string of the course content.

columns.name: block_content_hash
columns.description: str, sha256 hashed string of the block content in a course

columns.name: course_start
columns.description: timestamp, time indicating when the course starts, extracted from metadata of the 'course' block

columns.name: retrieved_at
columns.description: timestamp, time indicating when this block was initially retrieved from REST API.

columns.name: id
columns.description: int, the auto-incremented ID for this table

columns.name: student_id
columns.description: int, reference user id in auth_user from open edX

columns.name: course_id
columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID in the format course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag}

columns.name: module_id
columns.description: str, block ID for a distinct piece of content in a course, referencing course_structure

columns.name: module_type
columns.description: str, category/type of the block, referencing course_structure.

columns.name: state
columns.description: str, JSON text indicating the learner's last known state within a course.

columns.name: done
columns.description: str, not used. The value is 'na' in every row

columns.name: grade
columns.description: float, floating point value indicating the total unweighted grade for this problem that the learner has scored. e.g. how many responses they got right within the problem. This data is also available in courseactivity_problemcheck

columns.name: max_grade
columns.description: float, floating point value indicating the total possible unweighted grade for this problem, or basically the number of responses that are in this problem. This data is also available in courseactivity_problemcheck

columns.name: created
columns.description: timestamp, datetime when this row was created, which is typically when the learner first accesses this piece of content.

columns.name: modified
columns.description: timestamp, datetime when this row was last updated. A change in this field implies that there was a state change.

columns.name: id
columns.description: int, the auto-incremented ID for this table

columns.name: course_id
columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag}

columns.name: course_version
columns.description: str, The version number of the course in the Split Modulestore when the grade was computed. Currently used for debugging purposes only.

columns.name: usage_key
columns.description: str, block ID of the subsection (sequential) - foreign key to course_structure. e.g. block-v1:{org}+{course}+{run}type@{block type}+block@{hash code}

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, foreign key to auth_user from open edX platform

columns.name: visibleblocks_hash
columns.description: str, SHA1 hash of visible blocks within the subsection - foreign key to grades_visibleblocks. This field might be useful for identifying whether the course content was the same for two grades.

columns.name: possible_all
columns.description: float, the aggregated "total_weighted_possible" score in the subsection, calculated by summing all "weighted_possible" values of all (graded and ungraded) problems in the subsection.

columns.name: possible_graded
columns.description: float, The aggregated "total_weighted_possible" score in the subsection, calculated by summing all "weighted_possible" values of all "graded" problems in the subsection.

columns.name: earned_all
columns.description: float, The user's aggregated "total_weighted_earned" score in the subsection, calculated by summing all "weighted_earned" values of all (graded and ungraded) problems in the subsection.

columns.name: earned_graded
columns.description: float, The user's aggregated "total_weighted_earned" score in the subsection, calculated by summing all "weighted_earned" values of all "graded" problems in the subsection.

columns.name: first_attempted
columns.description: timestamp, time of the user's first attempt at a problem in the subsection.

columns.name: created
columns.description: timestamp, datetime of the subsection grade was first calculated for this user for this subsection.

columns.name: modified
columns.description: timestamp, datetime of the subsection grade was last updated for this user for this subsection. It will be updated every time the user attempts the problem.

columns.name: id
columns.description: int, the auto-incremented ID for this table

columns.name: grade_id
columns.description: int, foreign key to grades_persistentsubsectiongrade

columns.name: possible_all_override
columns.description: float, total score of all problems (both graded and ungraded) for the subsection

columns.name: possible_graded_override
columns.description: float, total score of all graded problems for the subsection

columns.name: earned_all_override
columns.description: float, total earned score (graded and ungraded) to be overridden.

columns.name: earned_graded_override
columns.description: float, override to learner's earned_graded score for the subsection

columns.name: override_reason
columns.description: str, instructor provided reason for override

columns.name: system
columns.description: str, indicating system where the override was performed. Possible values are grade-import, PROCTORING and GRADEBOOK

columns.name: id

columns.description: int, depth of a node in the tree. A root node has a depth of 1.

columns.description: int, the number of children of a node

columns.description: str, human-readable title of the page

columns.description: str, this is used for constructing the page’s URL.

columns.description: boolean, if set to true this page is published

columns.description: boolean, set to True when the page is either in draft or published with draft changes.

columns.description: str, alternate SEO-crafted title, for use in the page’s <title> HTML tag.

columns.name: url_path
columns.description: str, url path for the page

columns.description: boolean, toggles whether the page should be included in site-wide menus

columns.description: str, SEO-crafted description of the content, used for search indexing. This is also suitable for the page’s <meta name="description"> HTML tag.

columns.name: go_live_at
columns.description: timestamp, timestamp for when the page is scheduled to go live

columns.name: expire_at
columns.description: timestamp, timestamp for when the page is scheduled to be expired. This functionality is not used by xpro

columns.name: expired
columns.description: boolean, whether the page has expired. This functionality is not used by xpro

columns.description: int, primary key in django_contenttype. The cms_ table of that has data for this page

columns.name: owner_id
columns.description: int, primary key in users_user of the page owner

columns.name: locked
columns.description: boolean, when set to True, the Wagtail editor will not allow any users to edit the content of the page. This functionality is not used by xpro

columns.name: latest_revision_created_at
columns.description: timestamp, timestamp of the page's latest revision

columns.name: first_published_at
columns.description: timestamp, timestamp when the page was first published

columns.name: live_revision_id
columns.description: int, primary key in wagtailcore_pagerevision of the page's latest revision

columns.name: last_published_at
columns.description: timestamp, timestamp when the page was last published

columns.name: draft_title
columns.description: str, human-readable title of the page, incorporating any changes that have been made in a draft edit (in contrast to the title field, which for published pages will be the title as it exists in the current published version).

columns.description: int, Numerical user ID of a learner on a specific edX platform - MITx Online/xPro open edx or edx.org

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, The canonical ID of the course run in the format as {org}/{course}/{run}. This is old format and could be different from courses in MITxOnline or MicroMasters that use new format

columns.name: courserun_platform
columns.description: str, indicating what platform the data is from, MITx Online/xPro open edx or edx.org

columns.name: user_username
columns.description: str, username of a learner on the edX platform (some blank from source)

columns.name: user_gender
columns.description: str, user's gender - blank means user did not specify a gender. Null means this student signed up before this information was collected

columns.name: user_highest_education
columns.description: str, user's Level of education- blank means User did not specify level of education. Null means this student signed up before this information was collected

columns.name: user_birth_year
columns.description: int, A year of birth provided by the learner on their profile

columns.name: user_profile_country
columns.description: str, two-letter country code as specified by user in their profile (only available after ~mid-2014)

columns.name: courserunenrollment_enrollment_mode
columns.description: str, enrollment mode, e.g. honor, audit, verified

columns.name: courserunenrollment_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, timestamp of user's enrollment in the course

columns.name: courserunenrollment_enrolled_on
columns.description: timestamp, timestamp on when user initially had their first id-verified enrollment event

columns.name: courserunenrollment_unenrolled_on
columns.description: timestamp, timestamp on when user had their last id-verified unenrollment event, if any. Null if there were no unenrollment events

columns.name: courserunenrollment_is_active
columns.description: boolean, indicating if enrollment is active or not

columns.name: courseruncertificate_is_earned
columns.description: boolean, indicating that the user earned a certificate in the course

columns.name: courseruncertificate_status
columns.description: str, one of downloadable, notpassing, audit_passing, unavailable, audit_notpassing where downloadable should = earned

columns.name: courseruncertificate_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, timestamp when certificate was generated (if applicable)

columns.name: courseruncertificate_updated_on
columns.description: timestamp, timestamp when certificate was modified (if applicable)

columns.name: courserungrade_passing_grade
columns.description: float, Passing grade for the course, (range between 0.0 to 1.0)

columns.name: courserungrade_user_grade
columns.description: float, Final grade user earned in the course, (range between 0.0 to 1.0)

columns.name: courserungrade_is_passing
columns.description: boolean, indicating that the user completed the course with a passing grade

columns.name: courseactivitiy_visited_once
columns.description: boolean, indicating that the user visited the course at least once

columns.name: courseactivitiy_viewed_half
columns.description: boolean, indicating that the user viewed at least half of the chapters of the course

columns.name: courseactivitiy_num_events
columns.description: int, Number of tracking log events

columns.name: courseactivitiy_num_activity_days
columns.description: int, Number of days with activity from tracking logs

columns.name: courseactivitiy_num_progress_check
columns.description: int, Number of progress check events from tracking logs

columns.name: courseactivitiy_num_problem_check
columns.description: int, Number of problem_check interaction events from tracking logs

columns.name: courseactivitiy_num_show_answer
columns.description: int, Number of show_answer events from tracking logs

columns.name: courseactivitiy_num_show_transcript
columns.description: int, Number of show_transcript video interaction events from tracking logs

columns.name: courseactivitiy_num_seq_goto
columns.description: int, Number of sequence_goto navigational events from tracking logs

columns.name: courseactivitiy_num_play_video
columns.description: int, Number of play_video video interaction events from tracking logs

columns.name: courseactivitiy_num_seek_video
columns.description: int, Number of seek_video video interaction events from tracking logs

columns.name: courseactivitiy_num_pause_video
columns.description: int, Number of pause_video video interaction events from tracking logs

columns.name: courseactivitiy_num_video_interactions
columns.description: int, Number of all the video interaction events from tracking log

columns.name: courseactivitiy_num_unique_videos_viewed
columns.description: int, Number of Unique Videos viewed per user within a course

columns.name: courseactivitiy_percentage_total_videos_watched
columns.description: int, Percentage of all videos watched per user within a course

columns.name: courseactivitiy_average_time_diff_in_sec
columns.description: float, Average time difference in seconds between consecutive events from tracking logs

columns.name: courseactivitiy_standard_deviation_in_sec
columns.description: float, Standard deviation of difference in seconds between consecutive events from tracking logs

columns.name: courseactivitiy_max_diff_in_sec
columns.description: float, Maximum difference in seconds between consecutive events from tracking logs

columns.name: courseactivitiy_num_consecutive_events_used
columns.description: int, Number of consecutive events used in time difference computations, from tracking logs

columns.name: courseactivitiy_total_elapsed_time_in_sec
columns.description: int, Total elapsed time (in seconds) spent by user on this course, based on time difference of consecutive events, with 5 min max cutoff, from tracking logs

columns.name: courseactivitiy_first_event_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, timestamp of first event of user from tracking logs

columns.name: courseactivitiy_last_event_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, timestamp of last event of user from tracking logs

schema_snapshot.description: It contains user activities from edX.org, MITxOnline/xPro open edx and certain enrollment/certificate fields from edx.org

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, Numerical user ID of a learner on a specific edX platform - MITx Online/xPro open edx or edx.org

columns.name: courserun_platform
columns.description: str, indicating what platform the data is from, MITx Online/xPro open edx or edx.org. It could be null in this table

columns.name: user_username
columns.description: str, The username of the learner on the edX platform

columns.name: user_full_name
columns.description: str, The full name of the user on the edX platform

columns.name: user_email
columns.description: str, The email address of the learner on the edX platform (could be blank)

columns.name: user_map_hash_id
columns.description: str, A hashed ID mapping learner user ID's to external systems

columns.name: user_mailing_address
columns.description: str, Mailing address provided by the learner on their profile

columns.name: user_profile_goals
columns.description: str, The intended goals of the learner provided on their profile

columns.name: user_gender
columns.description: str, Gender selected by the user on their profile

columns.name: user_country
columns.description: str, Country selected by the learner on their profile

columns.name: user_city
columns.description: str, City selected the learner on their profile (Not currently used) History - Added in Jan 2014, not yet implemented.

columns.name: user_highest_education
columns.description: str, user's Level of education- blank means User did not specify level of education. Null means this student signed up before this information was collected

columns.name: user_birth_year
columns.description: int, A year of birth provided by the learner on their profile

columns.name: user_profile_meta
columns.description: str, extra metadata on learner's profile. JSON data in text field

columns.name: user_joined_on
columns.description: timestamp, timestamp that the account was created.

columns.name: user_last_login
columns.description: timestamp, Timestamp indicating the last time the user logged into the edX platform

columns.name: courserunenrollment_courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, course ID user is enrolling in. It's in the format as org/number/semester this field is not always populated here, but it's always populated in person_course

columns.name: courserunenrollment_mode
columns.description: str, indicate what kind of enrollment it is - mode, no-id-professional, audit, honor, verified, professional, credit

columns.name: courserunenrollment_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, Timestamp indicating when the enrollment event was created

columns.name: courserunenrollment_is_active
columns.description: int, Whether or not the enrollment is active. 1 means active

columns.name: courseruncertificate_id
columns.description: int, The ID of the certificate obtained by the user. Null if the user obtained no certificate.

columns.name: courseruncertificate_courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, The course ID associated with the certificate. It's in the format as org/number/semester

columns.name: courseruncertificate_user_id
columns.description: int, The user ID tied to the certificate

columns.name: courseruncertificate_mode
columns.description: str, The enrollment mode associated with the certificate

columns.name: courseruncertificate_distinction
columns.description: int, This was used for letters of distinction for 188.1x, but is not being used for any current courses

columns.name: courseruncertificate_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, Timestamp indicating when the certificate was created

columns.name: courseruncertificate_updated_on
columns.description: timestamp, Timestamp indicating the last time the certificate was modified

columns.name: courseruncertificate_name
columns.description: str, The name of the user that is listed on the certificate

columns.name: courseruncertificate_key
columns.description: str, A random string that is used to match server requests to responses sent to the LMS. Used internally only

columns.name: courseruncertificate_status
columns.description: str, Status of the generation of the certificate

columns.name: courseruncertificate_download_url
columns.description: str, the full URL to the certificate

columns.name: courseruncertificate_download_uuid
columns.description: str, A hash code that identifies this student’s certificate. Included as part of the download_url.

columns.name: courseruncertificate_verify_uuid
columns.description: str, A hash code that verifies the validity of a certificate. Included on the certificate itself as part of a URL.

columns.name: courseruncertificate_grade
columns.description: float, The grade when the certificate was generated

schema_snapshot.description: This contains users from edX.org, MITxOnline/xPro open edx sources and complete enrollments/certificates fields from edX.org

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, Numerical user ID of a learner on a specific edX platform - MITx Online/xPro open edx or edx.org

columns.name: user_username
columns.description: str, The username of the user on the edX platform

columns.name: user_email
columns.description: str, The email address of the user on the edX platform

columns.name: user_full_name
columns.description: str, The full name of the user on the edX platform

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, The course ID formatted as {org}/{course}/{run}

columns.name: courserun_platform
columns.description: str, indicating what platform the data is from, MITx Online/xPro open edx or edx.org

columns.name: user_is_opted_in_for_email
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the learner has opted to receive marketing emails

columns.name: user_email_opt_in_updated_on
columns.description: timestamp, specifying when email opt in preference was most recently updated

schema_snapshot.description: It contains email opt-in preferences for edX.org courses. If users enroll in multiple courses, the email and username in this table are expected to be the most recent data.

columns.name: org_id
columns.description: str, the name of the organization that is the author for the program for which this report was generated. Currently, it's MITx for all records.

columns.name: program_type
columns.description: str, the type of the program. The value are 'MicroMasters' and 'XSeries' for MITx programs on edX.org

columns.name: program_uuid
columns.description: str, the UUID of the program on edX.org. Each program and its separate track have a different UUID.

columns.name: program_title
columns.description: str, title of the program, including the specific track if applicable.

columns.name: program_track
columns.description: str, name of the specific track for MicroMasters programs. For example, Statistics and Data Science program has multiple tracks - General Track, Methods Track, Social Sciences Track, and 'Time Series and Social Sciences Track'.

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, the edX learner ID in the LMS in a user's course run enrollment record.

columns.name: user_username
columns.description: str, username of the user on edX.org

columns.name: user_full_name
columns.description: str, the full name of an edX learner in the LMS in a user's user profile record. Retired users will have no full name available.

columns.name: user_roles
columns.description: str, roles assigned to the user. A user can have multiple roles. Values are beta_testers, data_researcher, finance_admin, sales_admin, instructor, staff, etc.

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, the key of the course-run the learner has enrolled in, formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course}+{run}

columns.name: course_title
columns.description: str, the course run title on edX.org

columns.name: courserunenrollment_enrollment_mode
columns.description: str, the course mode of the learner's enrollment in this course-run. Also called enrollment mode. Values are 'audit', 'verified', 'honor' and 'credit' in this table.

columns.name: courserungrade_letter_grade
columns.description: str, the letter grade for the user in the course run. This value is based on the course run's grading policy. The letter grade may be blank if the grading/credentials task has not yet run for this learner. Values are 'A', 'B', 'C', 'D', 'F', 'Pass

columns.name: courserungrade_grade
columns.description: str, the current grade for the user in this course-run as a decimal (e.g. 0.84). If this value is null, the user does not yet have a course grade in this course-run.

columns.name: courserunenrollment_is_active
columns.description: boolean, whether the learner is currently enrolled in the course.

columns.name: user_has_purchased_as_bundle
columns.description: boolean, whether the learner has purchased this course as bundle.

columns.name: user_has_completed_program
columns.description: boolean, whether the learner has passed each of the courses in the program with a grade that qualifies them for a verified certificate.

columns.name: user_has_completed_course
columns.description: boolean, whether the learner completed the course run. true if a verified learner has earned a certificate in a course run that counts towards program completion. This is based on the user's course certificate in the LMS. A course certificate counts towards completion if the learner has passed the course and the course run mode matches the mode of the course. Learner certificates for unverified accounts do not count towards program completion until the account is verified.

columns.name: completed_course_on
columns.description: timestamp, the date in ISO-8601 format that the learner earned a passing certificate for this course run. If a user passes the course before verification, then the completed date will reflect the date the certificate was generated for that verified learner.

columns.name: program_certificate_awarded_on
columns.description: timestamp, the date in ISO-8601 format that the learner has passed each of the courses in the program and was awarded a program certificate. This is the first date that the learner was awarded a program certificate.

columns.name: courserun_start_on
columns.description: timestamp, the start date of the course run in ISO-8601 string.

columns.name: courserunenrollment_created_on
columns.description: timestamp,the date in ISO-8601 format that the learner enrolled in this course run for the first time. For example, if the learner enrolled in the course run, unenrolled, and then re-enrolled, this value would represent the time that they enrolled in the course run for the first time.

columns.name: courseactivity_last_activity_date
columns.description: date, the date in ISO-8601 format that the learner was last active in the course run.

columns.name: courserunenrollment_unenrolled_on
columns.description: timestamp, the date in ISO-8601 format that the learner last unenrolled in this course run. This will be null if the learner has never unenrolled from this course run. For example, if the learner enrolled in the course run, unenrolled, reenrolled, and then unenrolled, this value would represent the most recent time that they unenrolled in the course run.

columns.name: courserunenrollment_upgraded_on
columns.description: timestamp, the date in ISO-8601 format that the learner first upgraded to the verified track course mode.

schema_snapshot.description: learners program and course records in MicroMasters and XSeries programs from edX.org

columns.name: program_title
columns.description: title of the program that the learner purchased

columns.name: program_type
columns.description: type of the program. e.g., MicroMasters, XSeries

columns.name: organization_key
columns.description: the organization that offers the program. e.g., MITx

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: user ID on edX.org

columns.name: user_username
columns.description: username of the learner on edX.org

columns.name: user_email
columns.description: email address of the learner who purchased the program bundle

columns.name: purchase_date
columns.description: the date that the learner purchased the program bundle

columns.name: expiration_date
columns.description: the date that the entitlement expires

columns.name: number_of_entitlements
columns.description: number of courses that the learner has access to using the program bundle entitlement

columns.name: number_of_redeemed_entitlements
columns.description: number of courses in the program that the learner has redeemed access to using the program bundle entitlement

schema_snapshot.description: staging model for edX.org program entitlement records

columns.name: user_username
columns.description: str, username of a learner on edX.org

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, learner's user ID on edX.org. Extracted from context field.

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course}+{run} for course runs since Fall 2014, {org}/{course number}/{run} for older runs. Extracted from various fields - context.course_id, context.path, event_type and page. Note that the course ID extracted from context field may not be a valid. This field could be blank for any events that are not for any specific course .e.g. user login/out.

columns.name: org_id
columns.description: str, reference name in organizations_organization from open edX. e.g. MITx . Extracted from context field.

columns.name: useractivity_path
columns.description: str, URL that generated this event. Extracted from context field

columns.name: useractivity_context_object
columns.description: object, it includes member fields that provide contextual information. Common fields apply to all events are course_id, org_id, path, user_id. Other member fields for applicable events are course_user_tags, module.

columns.name: useractivity_event_source
columns.description: str, specifies the source of the interaction that triggered the event. The values are - browser, mobile, server, task

columns.name: useractivity_event_type
columns.description: str, type of event triggered. Values depend on event_source.

columns.name: useractivity_event_name
columns.description: str, type of event triggered. When this field is present for an event, it supersedes the event_type field.

columns.name: useractivity_event_object
columns.description: object,it includes member fields that identify specifics of each triggered event. Different member fields are supplied for different events.

columns.name: useractivity_page_url
columns.description: str, url of the page the user was visiting when the event was emitted.

columns.name: useractivity_session_id
columns.description: str, 32-character value to identify the user’s session. All browser events and the server 'enrollment' events include session value. Other server events and mobile events do not include a session value.

columns.name: useractivity_ip
columns.description: str, IP address of the user who triggered the event. Empty for mobile events.

columns.name: useractivity_http_host
columns.description: str, The site visited by the user. e.g. courses.edx.org

columns.name: useractivity_http_user_agent
columns.description: str, browser agent string of the user who triggered the event.

columns.name: useractivity_http_accept_language
columns.description: str, value from the HTTP Accept-Language request-header field

columns.name: useractivity_http_referer
columns.description: str, URI from the HTTP Referer request-header field

columns.name: useractivity_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, time at which the event was emitted, formatted as ISO 8601 string

schema_snapshot.description: edX.org event data that are emitted by server, the browser, or the mobile device to capture information about user's interactions with a course. This table filters out events that don't supply user identifiers like username and user_id.

columns.name: courserunenrollment_id
columns.description: int, primary key for course enrollment

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, open edX Course Key formatted as course-v1:org+course_number+run.

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, user ID on edX.org

columns.name: courserunenrollment_mode
columns.description: str, indicating what kind of enrollment it is. Possible values are honor, audit, credit, verified, unpaid-executive-education, professional, no-id-professional.

columns.name: courserunenrollment_is_active
columns.description: boolean, indicating whether this enrollment is active

columns.name: courserunenrollment_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when this enrollment was created

schema_snapshot.description: edX.org course run enrollments

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, open edX Course Key formatted as course-v1:org+course_number+run.

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, user ID on edX.org

columns.name: courserungrade_grading_policy_hash
columns.description: str, a SHA-1 digest of the course grading policy, used to detect and update grades whenever the policy changes

columns.name: courserungrade_grade
columns.description: float, calculated course grade as a decimal percentage, per grading policy. e.g. 0.91 means 91%

columns.name: courserungrade_letter_grade
columns.description: str, calculated course grade as a letter value (e.g., A-D, Pass), per grading policy If the learner's grade is Fail or F, this value is empty.

columns.name: courserungrade_first_passed_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the learner first passed the course. If this value is empty, the learner never passed the course. If this value is non-empty but the letter_grade value is empty, the learner transitioned from passing to not passing.

columns.name: courserungrade_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the course grade was first calculated for this user for this course run.

columns.name: courserungrade_updated_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the course grade was was last updated for this user for this course run

schema_snapshot.description: edx.org course grades ingested from CSV files on OL S3 bucket

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, course ID formatted as {org}/{course code}/{run_tag}

columns.name: coursestructure_content_hash
columns.description: str, sha256 hashed string of the course content.

columns.name: coursestructure_block_content_hash
columns.description: str, sha256 hashed string of the block content in a course

columns.name: coursestructure_block_id
columns.description: str, Unique ID for a distinct piece of content in a course, formatted as block-v1:{org}+{course}+{run}type@{block type}+block@{hash code}

columns.name: coursestructure_parent_block_id
columns.description: str, parent block ID, same format as block_id

columns.name: coursestructure_block_index
columns.description: int, sequence number giving order in which this block content appears within the course

columns.name: coursestructure_block_category
columns.description: str, category/type of the block, it identifies core structural elements of a course. Value includes but not limited to course, chapter, sequential, vertical, discussion, html, problem, video, etc.

columns.name: coursestructure_block_title
columns.description: str, title of the block extracted from the metadata of the block. This field comes from name field for the section, subsection, or unit on the Studio 'Course Outline' page.

columns.name: coursestructure_block_metadata
columns.description: str, json string of the metadata field for the block. It provides additional information about this block, different block type may have different member fields inside metadata.

columns.name: courserun_title
columns.description: str, title of the course extracted from the metadata of 'course' block

columns.name: coursestructure_retrieved_at
columns.description: timestamp, indicating when this course structure was initially retrieved from S3

schema_snapshot.description: This table captures the historical changes made to the edX.org course structure by comparing the new course block to the course block from the previous day. If no changes are detected, no new rows are added to the table. If there are any changes to the course structure, all blocks, including the updated or new ones, are added to the table.

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, primary key for this user table

columns.name: user_username
columns.description: str, the unique username for a user on edX.org.

columns.name: user_email
columns.description: str, the user’s email address, which is the primary mechanism users use to log in.

columns.name: user_is_staff
columns.description: boolean, set to true if the user is a staff member of edx, who have Admin privileges in the LMS

columns.name: user_is_active
columns.description: boolean, set to true if the user has clicked on the activation link that was sent to them when they created their account, and false if user hasn't activated the account or the account is soft deleted

columns.name: user_is_superuser
columns.description: boolean, controls access to django_admin views. Set to true only for site admins. false for almost everybody.

columns.name: user_last_login
columns.description: timestamp, date and time of the user’s last login. Should not be used as a proxy for activity, since people can use the site all the time and go days between logging in and out.

columns.name: user_joined_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time indicating when the account was created. This is not the date that the user activated the account.

schema_snapshot.description: edx.org user data

columns.name: user_profile_id
columns.description: int, primary key for user profile table

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, foreign key to auth_user.id

columns.name: user_full_name
columns.description: str, a user’s full name. Name changes are logged in the meta field.

columns.name: user_gender
columns.description: str, learner's gender in readable text

columns.name: user_highest_education
columns.description: str, learner's Level of education in readable text.

columns.name: user_year_of_birth
columns.description: int, learner's birth year.

columns.name: user_address_country
columns.description: str, two digit country code. Null for user profiles created before 18 Sep 2014.

columns.name: user_profile_metadata
columns.description: text, freeform text field that stores arbitrary JSON metadata e.g. old_names, old_emails for the user

columns.name: user_goals
columns.description: text, collected during registration to answer question "Tell us why you’re interested in edX

columns.name: user_bio
columns.description: text, one or more paragraphs of biographical information that the learner enters as profile information. Added 22 Apr 2015.

schema_snapshot.description: edx.org user profile data

columns.name: courseruncertificate_id
columns.description: int, primary key for certificates_generatedcertificate table

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, foreign key to auth_user.id

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, open edX Course ID formatted as {org}/{course number}/{run} for older runs prior to 3T2015, and course-v1:{org}+{course}+{run} for newer runs.

columns.name: courseruncertificate_status
columns.description: str, after certificates have been issued, the status will be one of the possible values- audit_notpassing, audit_passing, deleted, deleting, downloadable, error, generating, notpassing, restricted , unavailable, unverified

columns.name: courseruncertificate_download_url
columns.description: str, url location for the generated certificate. Used internally only.

columns.name: courseruncertificate_download_uuid
columns.description: str, the unique identifier for the generated certificate. Used internally only.

columns.name: courseruncertificate_verify_uuid
columns.description: str, a hash code that verifies the validity of a certificate. Included on the certificate itself as part of a URL.

columns.name: courseruncertificate_grade
columns.description: float, the grade ranged from '0' to '1' computed the at the time of certificate generation. It is suggested that the grades_persistentcoursegrade table be used instead of this grade.

columns.name: courseruncertificate_mode
columns.description: str, contains the value in student_courseenrollment.mode field at the time the certificate was generated

columns.name: courseruncertificate_user_full_name
columns.description: str, the name of the learner that was set at the time the certificate was generated.

columns.name: courseruncertificate_key
columns.description: str, used internally only.

columns.name: courseruncertificate_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time string indicating when the certificate was created

columns.name: courseruncertificate_updated_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time string indicating when the certificate was last modified

schema_snapshot.description: edx.org course runs certificate data

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, Numerical user ID of a learner on a specific edX platform - MITx Online/xPro open edx or edx.org
