columns.description: str, A hashed ID mapping learner user ID's to external systems

columns.name: is_staff
columns.description: int, Whether the user is a staff member of edx, 1 indicates staff member

columns.name: profile_language
columns.description: str, Language selected by the user as their default profile language (No longer used) History - User’s preferred language, asked during the sign up process for the 6.002x prototype course given in the Spring of 2012. Sometimes written in those languages. EdX stopped collecting this data after MITx transitioned to edX, but never removed the values for the first group of students.

columns.name: profile_location
columns.description: str, location chosen by the user on their profile (No longer used) History- User’s location, asked during the sign up process for the 6.002x prototype course given in the Spring of 2012. edX stopped collecting this column after MITx transitioned to edX, so it is only available for the first batch of students.

columns.name: profile_name
columns.description: str, The full name of the user on the edX platform

columns.name: profile_mailing_address
columns.description: str, Mailing address provided by the learner on their profile

columns.name: profile_allow_certificate
columns.description: int, Whether or not the profile allows certificate (No longer used) History - Prior to 10 Feb 2014, this field was set to 0 (false) if log analysis revealed that the student was accessing the edX site from a country that the U.S. had embargoed. This restriction is no longer in effect.

columns.name: profile_goals
columns.description: str, The intended goals of the learner provided on their profile

columns.name: profile_gender
columns.description: str, Gender selected by the user on their profile

columns.name: profile_country
columns.description: str, Country selected by the learner on their profile

columns.name: profile_level_of_education
columns.description: str, user's Level of education- blank means User did not specify level of education. Null means this student signed up before this information was collected

columns.name: profile_year_of_birth
columns.description: int, A year of birth provided by the learner on their profile

columns.name: profile_meta
columns.description: str, extra metadata on learner's profile. JSON data in text field

columns.name: profile_courseware
columns.description: str, The courseware under which the profile was created History - At one point, it was part of a way to do A/B tests, but it has not been used for anything meaningful since the conclusion of the prototype course in the spring of 2012.

columns.name: profile_city
columns.description: str, City selected the learner on their profile (Not currently used) History - Added in Jan 2014, not yet implemented.

columns.name: date_joined
columns.description: timestamp, timestamp that the account was created.

columns.name: last_login
columns.description: timestamp, Timestamp indicating the last time the user logged into the edX platform

columns.name: enrollment_course_id
columns.description: str, course ID user is enrolling in. It's in the format as org/number/semester

columns.name: enrollment_mode
columns.description: str, indicate what kind of enrollment it is - mode, no-id-professional, audit, honor, verified, professional, credit

columns.name: enrollment_created
columns.description: timestamp, Timestamp indicating when the enrollment event was created

columns.name: enrollment_is_active
columns.description: int, Whether or not the enrollment is active. 1 means active

columns.name: certificate_id
columns.description: int, The ID of the certificate obtained by the user. Null if the user obtained no certificate.

columns.name: certificate_course_id
columns.description: str, The course ID associated with the certificate. It's in the format as org/number/semester

columns.name: certificate_user_id
columns.description: int, The user ID tied to the certificate

columns.name: certificate_mode
columns.description: str, The enrollment mode associated with the certificate

columns.name: certificate_distinction
columns.description: int, This was used for letters of distinction for 188.1x, but is not being used for any current courses

columns.name: certificate_created_date
columns.description: timestamp, Timestamp indicating when the certificate was created

columns.name: certificate_modified_date
columns.description: timestamp, Timestamp indicating the last time the certificate was modified

columns.name: certificate_name
columns.description: str, The name of the user that is listed on the certificate

columns.name: certificate_key
columns.description: str, A random string that is used to match server requests to responses sent to the LMS. Used internally only

columns.name: certificate_status
columns.description: str, Status of the generation of the certificate

columns.name: certificate_download_url
columns.description: str, the full URL to the certificate

columns.name: certificate_download_uuid
columns.description: str, A hash code that identifies this student’s certificate. Included as part of the download_url.

columns.name: certificate_verify_uuid
columns.description: str, A hash code that verifies the validity of a certificate. Included on the certificate itself as part of a URL.

columns.name: certificate_grade
columns.description: float, The grade when the certificate was generated

columns.name: certificate_error_reason
columns.description: str, indicate any errors encountered during certificate generation. Used internally only.

columns.name: edxinstructordash_grade
columns.description: float, A grade downloaded separately from the instructor dashboards. This is mostly null for all tables generated by the simeon CLI tool version 0.0.1

columns.name: edxinstructordash_grade_timestamp
columns.description: str, Timestamp indicating when the grades from the instructor dashboards were generated

columns.name: y1_anomalous
columns.description: int, relic from processes that the edx2bigquery package used to handle

columns.name: course_id
columns.description: str, The canonical ID of the course in the format as {org}/{course}/{run}.

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: int, Numerical user ID of a learner on a specific edX platform - MITx Online/xPro open edx or edx.org

columns.name: username
columns.description: str, username of a learner on the edX platform

columns.name: email
columns.description: str, The email address of the learner on the edX platform

columns.name: full_name
columns.description: str, The full name of the user on the edX platform

columns.name: is_opted_in_for_email
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the learner has opted to receive marketing emails

columns.name: preference_set_datetime
columns.description: timestamp, specifying when email opt in preference was most recently updated

columns.name: authoring institution
columns.description: str, the name of the organization that is the author for the program for which this report was generated. Currently, it's MITx for all records.

columns.name: program type
columns.description: str, the type of the program. The value are 'MicroMasters' and 'XSeries' for MITx programs on edX.org

columns.name: program uuid
columns.description: str, the UUID of the program on edX.org. 'MIT Finance' and 'Finance' are two different UUIDs, as they have different required courses. 'Statistics and Data Science' is split into 'Statistics and Data Science' and 'Statistics and Data Science (General track)', which are also two different program UUIDs

columns.name: program title
columns.description: str, title of the program on edX.org

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: str, the edX learner ID in the LMS in a user's course run enrollment record.

columns.name: username
columns.description: str, username of the user on edX.org

columns.name: name
columns.description: str, The full name of an edX learner in the LMS in a user's user profile record. Retired users will have no full name available.

columns.name: user roles
columns.description: str, roles assigned to the user. A user can have multiple roles. Values are beta_testers, data_researcher, finance_admin, sales_admin, instructor, staff, etc.

columns.name: course run key
columns.description: str, the key of the course-run the learner has enrolled in, formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course}+{run}

columns.name: course title
columns.description: str, the course run title on edX.org

columns.name: track
columns.description: str, the course mode of the learner's enrollment in this course-run. Also called enrollment mode. Values are 'audit', 'verified', 'honor' and 'credit' in this table.

columns.name: letter grade
columns.description: str, the letter grade for the user in the course run. This value is based on the course run's grading policy. The letter grade may be blank if the grading/credentials task has not yet run for this learner. Values are 'A', 'B', 'C', 'D', 'F', 'Pass'.

columns.name: grade
columns.description: str, the current grade for the user in this course-run as a decimal (e.g. 0.84). If this value is null, the user does not yet have a course grade in this course-run.

columns.name: currently enrolled
columns.description: str, whether the learner is currently enrolled in the course. 'true' or 'false

columns.name: purchased as bundle
columns.description: str, whether the learner has purchased this course as bundle. 'true' or 'false

columns.name: completed
columns.description: str, whether the learner completed the course run. 'true' if a verified learner has earned a certificate in a course run that counts towards program completion. This is based on the user's course certificate in the LMS. A course certificate counts towards completion if the learner has passed the course and the course run mode matches the mode of the course. Learner certificates for unverified accounts do not count towards program completion until the account is verified.

columns.name: completed program
columns.description: str, whether the learner has passed each of the courses in the program with a grade that qualifies them for a verified certificate. 'true' or 'false'.

columns.name: date completed
columns.description: str, the date in the format 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z' in UTC that the learner earned a passing certificate for this course run. If a user passes the course before verification, then the completed date will reflect the date the certificate was generated for that verified learner.

columns.name: date program certificate awarded
columns.description: str, the date in the format 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ' in UTC that the learner has passed each of the courses in the program and was awarded a program certificate.

columns.name: last activity date
columns.description: str, the date in the format 'YYYY-MM-DD' in UTC that the learner was last active in the course run.

columns.name: date first enrolled
columns.description: str, the date in the format 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z' in UTC that the learner enrolled in this course run for the first time. For example, if the learner enrolled in the course run, unenrolled, and then re-enrolled, this value would represent the time that they enrolled in the course run for the first time.

columns.name: date last unenrolled
columns.description: str, the date in the format 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z' in UTC that the learner last unenrolled in this course run. This will be null if the learner has never unenrolled from this course run. For example, if the learner enrolled in the course run, unenrolled, reenrolled, and then unenrolled, this value would represent the most recent time that they unenrolled in the course run.

columns.name: date first upgraded to verified
columns.description: str, the date in the format 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z' in UTC that the learner first upgraded to the verified track course mode.

columns.name: course run start date
columns.description: str, the start date of the course run in the format 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z' in UTC

columns.name: username
columns.description: str, username of the open edX user who caused the event to be emitted. Some events are recorded with a blank username. This can occur when a user logs out, or the login session times out, while a browser window remains open. EdX recommends to ignore these events.

columns.name: context
columns.description: object, it includes member fields that provide contextual information. Common fields apply to all events are course_id, org_id, path, user_id. Other member fields for applicable events are course_user_tags, module.

columns.name: event_source
columns.description: str, specifies the source of the interaction that triggered the event. The values are - browser, mobile, server, task

columns.name: event_type
columns.description: str, type of event triggered. Values depend on event_source.

columns.name: name
columns.description: str, type of event triggered. When this field is present for an event, it supersedes the event_type field.

columns.name: event
columns.description: object, it includes member fields that identify specifics of each triggered event. Different member fields are supplied for different events.

columns.name: page
columns.description: str, url of the page the user was visiting when the event was emitted.

columns.name: session
columns.description: str, 32-character value to identify the user’s session. All browser events and the server 'enrollment' events include a session value. Other server events and mobile events do not include a session value.

columns.name: ip
columns.description: str, IP address of the user who triggered the event. Empty for mobile events.

columns.name: host
columns.description: str, the site visited by the user. e.g. courses.edx.org

columns.name: time
columns.description: str, time at which the event was emitted in YYYY-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSSSSS format

columns.name: agent
columns.description: str, browser agent string of the user who triggered the event.

columns.name: accept_language
columns.description: str, value from the HTTP Accept-Language request-header field

columns.name: referer
columns.description: str, URI from the HTTP Referer request-header field

columns.name: log_file
columns.description: str, internal used field for log file location

columns.name: _ab_source_file_url
columns.description: str, url path for the raw log file

columns.name: id
columns.description: str, primary key for course run enrollment

columns.name: course_id
columns.description: str, open edX Course Key formatted as course-v1:org+course_number+run.

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: str, user ID on edX.org

columns.name: mode
columns.description: str, indicating what kind of enrollment it is. Possible values are honor, audit, credit, verified, unpaid-executive-education, professional, no-id-professional.

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: str, indicating whether this enrollment is active

columns.name: created
columns.description: str, time when this enrollment was created

columns.name: course_id
columns.description: str, open edX Course Key formatted as course-v1:org+course_number+run.

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: str, user ID on edX.org

columns.name: grading_policy_hash
columns.description: str, a SHA-1 digest of the course grading policy, used to detect and update grades whenever the policy changes

columns.name: percent_grade
columns.description: str, calculated course grade as a decimal percentage, per grading policy. e.g. 0.91 means 91%

columns.name: letter_grade
columns.description: str, calculated course grade as a letter value (e.g., A-D, Pass), per grading policy If the learner's grade is Fail or F, this value is empty.

columns.name: passed_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the learner first passed the course. If this value is empty, the learner never passed the course. If this value is non-empty but the letter_grade value is empty, the learner transitioned from passing to not passing.

columns.name: created
columns.description: str, time when the course grade was first calculated for this user for this course run.

columns.name: modified
columns.description: str, time when the course grade was was last updated for this user for this course run

columns.name: block_id
columns.description: str, Unique ID for a distinct piece of content in a course, formatted as block-v1:{org}+{course}+{run}type@{block type}+block@{hash code}

columns.name: block_index
columns.description: int, sequence number giving order in which this block content appears within the course

columns.name: block_parent
columns.description: str, parent block ID, same format as block_id field

columns.name: block_type
columns.description: str, category/type of the block, it identifies core structural elements of a course. Value includes but not limited to course, chapter, sequential, vertical, discussion, html, problem, video, etc.

columns.name: block_title
columns.description: str, title of the block extracted from the metadata field of the block. This field comes from name field for the section, subsection, or unit on the Studio 'Course Outline' page

columns.name: block_details
columns.description: str, json string of the block. It contains the attribute fields like category, children and metadata. Different block type may have different member fields inside metadata.

columns.name: course_id
columns.description: str, course ID wrapped in quotes. e.g., ["MITx-CTL.SC1x_1-3T2022"]

columns.name: course_title
columns.description: str, title of the course extracted from the metadata field of 'course' block

columns.name: course_content_hash
columns.description: str, sha256 hashed string of the course content.

columns.name: block_content_hash
columns.description: str, sha256 hashed string of the block content in a course

columns.name: course_start
columns.description: str, date indicating when the course starts, extracted from metadata of the 'course' block

columns.name: retrieved_at
columns.description: timestamp, time indicating when this block was initially retrieved from S3

columns.name: id
columns.description: str, primary key for this user table

columns.name: username
columns.description: str, the unique username for a user on edX.org.

columns.name: first_name
columns.description: str, not used; a user’s full name is stored in auth_userprofile.name instead.

columns.name: last_name
columns.description: str, not used; a user’s full name is stored in auth_userprofile.name instead.

columns.name: email
columns.description: str, the user’s email address, which is the primary mechanism users use to log in.

columns.name: password
columns.description: str, the hashed version of the user’s password

columns.name: is_staff
columns.description: str, set to '1' if the user is a staff member of edx, who have Admin privileges in the LMS

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: str, set to '1' if the user has clicked on the activation link that was sent to them when they created their account, and false if user hasn't activated the account or the account is soft deleted

columns.name: is_superuser
columns.description: str, controls access to django_admin views. Set to '1' only for site admins. '0' for almost everybody.

columns.name: last_login
columns.description: str, date and time string of the user’s last login. Should not be used as a proxy for activity, since people can use the site all the time and go days between logging in and out.

columns.name: date_joined
columns.description: str, date and time string indicating when the account was created. This is not the date that the user activated the account.

columns.name: status
columns.description: str, obsolete column that was added by an application called Askbot, a discussion forum package that is no longer part of the system. Only users who were part of the prototype 6.002x course run in Spring 2012 may have any information in these columns.

columns.name: email_key
columns.description: str, obsolete column that was added by an application called Askbot, a discussion forum package that is no longer part of the system. Only users who were part of the prototype 6.002x course run in Spring 2012 may have any information in these columns.

columns.name: avatar_type
columns.description: str, obsolete column that was added by an application called Askbot, a discussion forum package that is no longer part of the system. Only users who were part of the prototype 6.002x course run in Spring 2012 may have any information in these columns.

columns.name: country
columns.description: str, obsolete column that was added by an application called Askbot, a discussion forum package that is no longer part of the system. Only users who were part of the prototype 6.002x course run in Spring 2012 may have any information in these columns.

columns.name: show_country
columns.description: str, obsolete column that was added by an application called Askbot, a discussion forum package that is no longer part of the system. Only users who were part of the prototype 6.002x course run in Spring 2012 may have any information in these columns.

columns.name: date_of_birth
columns.description: str, obsolete column that was added by an application called Askbot, a discussion forum package that is no longer part of the system. Only users who were part of the prototype 6.002x course run in Spring 2012 may have any information in these columns.

columns.name: interesting_tags
columns.description: str, obsolete column that was added by an application called Askbot, a discussion forum package that is no longer part of the system. Only users who were part of the prototype 6.002x course run in Spring 2012 may have any information in these columns.

columns.name: ignored_tags
columns.description: str, obsolete column that was added by an application called Askbot, a discussion forum package that is no longer part of the system. Only users who were part of the prototype 6.002x course run in Spring 2012 may have any information in these columns.

columns.name: email_tag_filter_strategy
columns.description: str, obsolete column that was added by an application called Askbot, a discussion forum package that is no longer part of the system. Only users who were part of the prototype 6.002x course run in Spring 2012 may have any information in these columns.

columns.name: display_tag_filter_strategy
columns.description: str, obsolete column that was added by an application called Askbot, a discussion forum package that is no longer part of the system. Only users who were part of the prototype 6.002x course run in Spring 2012 may have any information in these columns.

columns.name: consecutive_days_visit_count
columns.description: str, obsolete column that was added by an application called Askbot, a discussion forum package that is no longer part of the system. Only users who were part of the prototype 6.002x course run in Spring 2012 may have any information in these columns.

columns.name: id
columns.description: str, primary key for user profile table

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: str, foreign key to auth_user.id

columns.name: name
columns.description: str, a user’s full name. Name changes are logged in the meta field.

columns.name: meta
columns.description: str, freeform text field that stores arbitrary JSON metadata e.g. old_names, old_emails for the user

columns.name: gender
columns.description: str, possible values are m, f, o, t, b, blank, or NULL

columns.name: level_of_education
columns.description: str, learner's Level of education in one or two letters.

columns.name: year_of_birth
columns.description: str, learner's birth year.

columns.name: goals
columns.description: text, collected during registration to answer question "Tell us why you’re interested in edX

columns.name: country
columns.description: str, two digit country code. Null for user profiles created before 18 Sep 2014.

columns.name: bio
columns.description: text, one or more paragraphs of biographical information that the learner enters as profile information. Added 22 Apr 2015.

columns.name: profile_image_uploaded_at
columns.description: str, the date and time when a learner uploaded a profile image to show with profile information. Added 22 Apr 2015.

columns.name: city
columns.description: str, not currently used. NULL for all user profiles except a few rows that may have incomplete or bad data

columns.name: language
columns.description: str, no longer used. User’s preferred language, asked during the sign up process for the 6.002x prototype course given in Spring 2012. EdX stopped collecting this data after MITx transitioned to edX

columns.name: location
columns.description: str, no longer used. User’s location, asked during the sign up process for the 6.002x prototype course given in Spring 2012. Like language, edX stopped collecting this column after MITx transitioned to edX, so it is only available for the first batch of learners.

columns.name: courseware
columns.description: str, no longer used. This column was added for use with an A/B testing feature, but it has not been used for anything meaningful since the prototype course concluded in Spring 2012.

columns.name: mailing_address
columns.description: str, no longer used. This column contains NULL for learners who register after 17 May 2016 as well as for learners who registered accounts for the prototype course.

columns.name: id

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: str, foreign key to auth_user.id

columns.name: course_id

columns.name: download_url
columns.description: str, url location for the generated certificate. Used internally only.

columns.name: download_uuid

columns.name: verify_uuid
columns.description: str, a hash code that verifies the validity of a certificate. Included on the certificate itself as part of a URL.

columns.name: grade

columns.name: mode

columns.name: status
columns.description: str, after certificates have been issued, the status will be one of the possible values- audit_notpassing, audit_passing, deleted, deleting, downloadable, error, generating, notpassing, restricted , unavailable, unverified

columns.name: error_reason
columns.description: st, used internally only

columns.name: distinction
columns.description: str, not used. currently all '0

columns.name: name

columns.name: key
columns.description: str, used internally only.

columns.name: created_date

columns.name: modified_date
columns.description: str, date and time string indicating when the certificate was last modified

columns.name: course_id
columns.description: str, open edX Course ID formatted as {org}/{course number}/{run} for older runs prior to 3T2015, and course-v1:{org}+{course}+{run} for newer runs.

columns.name: org

columns.name: role
columns.description: str, the privilege level assigned to the user for working in this course. Possible values are instructor, staff, beta_testers, data_researcher, ccx_coach, limited_staff, sales_admin, beta_testers, finance_admin

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: str, foreign key to auth_user.id

columns.name: course_number
columns.description: str, unique string for the course. It can contain letters, numbers, or periods. e.g. CTL.SC2x

columns.name: semester

columns.name: title

columns.name: course_start
columns.description: str, specifying when the course begins

columns.name: course_end

columns.name: enrollment_start

columns.name: enrollment_end

columns.name: instructor_info

columns.name: id

columns.name: student_id
columns.description: str, reference user id in auth_user from open edX

columns.name: course_id
columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID in the format course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag}

columns.name: module_id
columns.description: str, block ID for a distinct piece of content in a course, referencing course_structure

columns.name: module_type
columns.description: str, category/type of the block, referencing course_structure.

columns.name: state
columns.description: str, JSON text indicating the learner's last known state within a course.

columns.name: done
columns.description: str, not used. The value is 'na' in every row

columns.name: grade
columns.description: str, floating point value indicating the total unweighted grade for this problem that the learner has scored. e.g. how many responses they got right within the problem. This data is also available in courseactivity_problemcheck

columns.name: max_grade
columns.description: str, floating point value indicating the total possible unweighted grade for this problem, or basically the number of responses that are in this problem. This data is also available in courseactivity_problemcheck

columns.name: created
columns.description: timestamp, datetime when this row was created, which is typically when the learner first accesses this piece of content.

columns.name: modified
columns.description: timestamp, datetime when this row was last updated. A change in this field implies that there was a state change.

columns.name: courserun_id
columns.description: str, edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course code}+{run_tag}

columns.name: video_block_id
columns.description: str, hash code or module name for the video. This value is the last part of video block ID string, e.g., block-v1:{org}+{course}+{run}type@{block type}+block@{module name or hash code}.

columns.name: edx_video_id
columns.description: str, video ID on the edX platform. This value is the same as the edx_video_id field in the coursestructure_block_metadata JSON for the video block.

columns.name: duration
columns.description: float, the length of the video, in seconds. May be 0.0.

columns.name: data_modified_timestamp

columns.name: username
columns.description: str, username of the open edX user who caused the event to be emitted. Some events are recorded with a blank username. This can occur when a user logs out, or the login session times out, while a browser window remains open. EdX recommends to ignore these events.

columns.name: context
columns.description: object, it includes member fields that provide contextual information. Common fields apply to all events are course_id, org_id, path, user_id. Other member fields for applicable events are course_user_tags, module.

columns.name: event_source
columns.description: str, specifies the source of the interaction that triggered the event. The values are - browser, mobile, server, task

columns.name: event_type
columns.description: str, type of event triggered. Values depend on event_source.

columns.name: name
columns.description: str, type of event triggered. When this field is present for an event, it supersedes the event_type field.

columns.name: event
columns.description: object, it includes member fields that identify specifics of each triggered event. Different member fields are supplied for different events.

columns.name: page
columns.description: str, url of the page the user was visiting when the event was emitted.

columns.name: session
columns.description: str, 32-character value to identify the user’s session. All browser events and the server 'enrollment' events include a session value. Other server events and mobile events do not include a session value.

columns.name: ip
columns.description: str, IP address of the user who triggered the event. Empty for mobile events.

columns.name: host
columns.description: str, the site visited by the user. e.g. courses.xpro.mit.edu

columns.name: time
columns.description: str, time at which the event was emitted. It has inconsistent formats due to log collector switches, YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss.SSSSSS for older records, YYYY-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSSSS for newer records

columns.name: agent
columns.description: str, browser agent string of the user who triggered the event.

columns.name: accept_language
columns.description: str, value from the HTTP Accept-Language request-header field

columns.name: referer
columns.description: str, URI from the HTTP Referer request-header field

columns.name: log_file
columns.description: str, internal used field for log file location

columns.name: _ab_source_file_url
columns.description: str, url path for the raw log file

columns.name: vector_timestamp
columns.description: str, time when Vector processed this record. Blank for old records.

columns.name: environment
columns.description: str, internal used field to indicate environment for the event. e.g. xpro-production

columns.name: possible_all

columns.name: possible_graded

columns.name: earned_all

columns.name: created
columns.description: timestamp, datetime of the subsection grade was first calculated for this user for this subsection.

columns.name: modified
columns.description: timestamp, datetime of the subsection grade was last updated for this user for this subsection. It will be updated every time the user attempts the problem.

columns.description: str, calculated course grade as a letter value (e.g., A-D, Pass), per grading policy If the learner's grade is Fail or F, this value is empty.

columns.name: passed_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the learner first passed the course. If this value is empty, the learner never passed the course. If this value is non-empty but the letter_grade value is empty, the learner transitioned from passing to not passing.

columns.name: course_edited_timestamp

schema_snapshot.description: The ecommerce_couponpaymentversion holds information about a batch of coupons. A coupon payment version has information about the dollar or percentage amount of the discount, the number of available coupons that can be used to redeem the discount, how many times they can be redeemed and the source of the coupons. Coupon groups can be generated internally, for example to allow staff to enroll in courses without paying or marketing to create promos that allow learners to take a percentage or dollar amount off the full course price. Other coupon groups are created when a company buys bulk seats in a course for their employees, which generates a batch of 100% off coupons that learners can use to enroll in a course or program.

columns.name: user_username
columns.description: str, username of the open edX user who caused the event to be emitted.

columns.name: openedx_user_id
columns.description: int, reference user id in auth_user from open edX. Extracted from context field.

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:{org}+{course number}+{run_tag}. Extracted from various fields - context.course_id, context.path, event_type and page. The course ID extracted from context field may not be valid, it would require joining with courserun table to be sure. This field could be blank for any events that are not for any specific course .e.g. user login/out, visiting dashboard, some course team events e.g. course export from studio

columns.name: org_id
columns.description: str, reference name in organizations_organization from open edX. e.g. xPRO . Extracted from context field

columns.name: useractivity_path
columns.description: str, URL that generated this event. Extracted from context field

columns.name: useractivity_context_object
columns.description: object, it includes member fields that provide contextual information. Common fields apply to all events are course_id, org_id, path, user_id. Other member fields for applicable events are course_user_tags, module.

columns.name: useractivity_event_source
columns.description: str, specifies the source of the interaction that triggered the event. The values are - browser, mobile, server, task

columns.name: useractivity_event_type
columns.description: str, type of event triggered. Values depend on event_source.

columns.name: useractivity_event_name
columns.description: str, type of event triggered. When this field is present for an event, it supersedes the event_type field.

columns.name: useractivity_event_object
columns.description: object,it includes member fields that identify specifics of each triggered event. Different member fields are supplied for different events.

columns.name: useractivity_page_url
columns.description: str, url of the page the user was visiting when the event was emitted.

columns.name: useractivity_session_id
columns.description: str, 32-character value to identify the user’s session. All browser events and the server 'enrollment' events include session value. Other server events and mobile events do not include a session value.

columns.name: useractivity_ip
columns.description: str, IP address of the user who triggered the event. Empty for mobile events.

columns.name: useractivity_http_host
columns.description: str, The site visited by the user. e.g. courses.xpro.mit.edu

columns.name: useractivity_http_user_agent
columns.description: str, browser agent string of the user who triggered the event.

columns.name: useractivity_http_accept_language
columns.description: str, value from the HTTP Accept-Language request-header field

columns.name: useractivity_http_referer
columns.description: str, URI from the HTTP Referer request-header field

columns.name: useractivity_timestamp
columns.description: timestamp, time at which the event was emitted, formatted as ISO 8601 string

schema_snapshot.description: This table is deduped as raw table has duplicate events. It filters out blank username events since these don't supply user identifiers and also those server "exception" events due to server errors

columns.name: courserun_readable_id
columns.description: str, Open edX Course ID formatted as course-v1:xPRO+{course code}+{run_tag}

columns.name: coursestructure_content_hash
columns.description: str, sha256 hashed string of the course content.

columns.name: coursestructure_block_content_hash
columns.description: str, sha256 hashed string of the block content in a course

columns.name: coursestructure_block_id
columns.description: str, Unique ID for a distinct piece of content in a course, formatted as block-v1:xPRO+{course}+{run}type@{block type}+block@{hash code}

columns.name: coursestructure_parent_block_id
columns.description: str, parent block ID, same format as block_id

columns.name: coursestructure_block_index
columns.description: int, sequence number giving order in which this block content appears within the course

columns.name: coursestructure_block_category
columns.description: str, category/type of the block, it identifies core structural elements of a course. Value includes but not limited to course, chapter, sequential, vertical, discussion, html, problem, video, etc.

columns.name: coursestructure_block_title
columns.description: str, title of the block extracted from the metadata of the block. This field comes from name field for the section, subsection, or unit on the Studio 'Course Outline' page.

columns.name: coursestructure_block_metadata
columns.description: str, json string of the metadata field for the block. It provides additional information about this block, different block type may have different member fields inside metadata.

columns.name: courserun_title
columns.description: str, title of the course extracted from the metadata of 'course' block

columns.name: courserun_start_on
columns.description: timestamp, indicating when the course run starts extracted from the metadata of 'course' block

columns.name: coursestructure_retrieved_at
columns.description: timestamp, indicating when this course structure was initially retrieved from REST API.

schema_snapshot.description: This table captures the historical changes made to the xPro course structure by comparing the new course block to the course block from the previous day. If no changes are detected, no new rows are added to the table. If there are any changes to the course structure, all blocks, including the updated or new ones, are added to the table.

columns.name: studentmodule_updated_on

columns.name: id
columns.description: string, globally unique string that identifies an opportunity

columns.name: name
columns.description: string, name for the opportunity

columns.name: type
columns.description: string, opportunity type - Existing Business, Expansion Business, New Business

columns.name: description
columns.description: string, text description of the opportunity

columns.name: business_type__c
columns.description: string, indicating business type - B2C (Business to Consumer), B2B (Business to Business) or B2X (Business-to-Exchange). B2X is inactive.

columns.name: self_pay__c
columns.description: boolean, indicating that learner intends to or has paid for their enrollment code(s) with a credit card. It's based on Agreement Type dropdown choice

columns.name: agreement_type__c
columns.description: string, type of Agreement. Possible values - 3rd Party Hosted, Additional MSA Order Form, Affiliate Direct, Affiliate via Intermediary, Bulk Sales, Content Licensing, Master Service Agreement, Membership, Private Course (SPOC), Reseller, Self-Pay Opp, Subscription, Training Broker, Underwriting, Workshop

columns.name: desired_implementation_date__c
columns.description: date, desired implementation date

columns.name: revenue_share_or_commission_contract__c
columns.description: boolean, indicating if MIT will have to pay a fee or commission to another party because of this opportunity

columns.name: revenue_share_or_commission_amount__c
columns.description: decimal, actual $ amount of revenue share/commission once it has been calculated for this opportunity

columns.name: mit_is_sales_lead__c
columns.description: boolean, indicating if MIT is the lead (or unsupported sales) in opportunity for 3rd party hosted sales

columns.name: stagename
columns.description: string, current stage of the opportunity. Some examples - Closed Won, Closed Won, Enrollment List, Contracting, Demo, Order, Prospecting, Connecting, etc

columns.name: xpro_bulk_code_payment_link__c
columns.description: string, xPRO Bulk Code Payment Link for customers who want to pay with credit cards for enrollment codes

columns.name: enrollment_codes_redeemed__c
columns.description: int, sum of enrollment codes redeemed

columns.name: hasopenactivity
columns.description: boolean, indicating if this opportunity has open activity

columns.name: requires_icc_review__c
columns.description: boolean, indicating if this opportunity requires ICC review (legacy)

columns.name: icc_reviewed__c
columns.description: boolean, indicating if this opportunity is ICC reviewed

columns.name: leadsource
columns.description: string, Source of the opportunity, such as Advertisement, Partner, or Web

columns.name: hasopportunitylineitem
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the opportunity has product line item

columns.name: amount
columns.description: decimal, estimated total sale amount in US dollar

columns.name: probability
columns.description: int, percentage of the likelihood that opportunity will close - 0 to 100 0 means Closed/Lost, 100 means Closed/Won

columns.name: expectedrevenue
columns.description: decimal, calculated revenue based on the Amount and Probability fields

columns.name: iswon
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the opportunity is a closed-Won sale

columns.name: isclosed
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the opportunity is a closed sale, which can be Closed-Lost or Closed-Won

columns.name: issplit
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the opportunity is split between team members

columns.name: closedate
columns.description: date, close date for the opportunity

columns.name: mm_dedp_opportunity__c
columns.description: boolean, indicating if it's MicroMasters DEDP Opportunity. Check this box for OL to perform invoice and collections tasks. If unchecked assumed that learner will pay edX.

columns.name: of_seats_assigned_by_company__c
columns.description: int, number of Seats Assigned by Company

columns.name: of_seats_outstanding_for_company__c
columns.description: int, number of Seats Outstanding for Company

columns.name: of_seats_purchased__c
columns.description: int, number of Seats purchased

columns.name: price_per_seat__c
columns.description: int, price per seat, it's calculated Amount / of_Seats_Purchased__c in US dollar

columns.name: forecastcategory
columns.description: string, category that determines the column in which an opportunity is totaled in a forecast - Forecast, Closed, Pipeline, Omitted, BestCase, MostLikely

columns.name: forecastcategoryname
columns.description: string, The category within the sales cycle that an opportunity is assigned to based on its opportunity stage. The standard forecast categories are Pipeline, Best Case, Commit, Omitted, and Closed

columns.name: data_quality_score__c
columns.description: int, data quality score between 0 to 100

columns.name: data_quality_description__c
columns.description: string, description for data quality score

columns.name: horizon_enterprise_opp__c
columns.description: boolean, indicating if Opp is for Enterprise subscription

columns.name: nextstep
columns.description: string, next step noted for this opportunity

columns.name: hasoverduetask
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the opportunity has OverDue task

columns.name: opportunity_product_total__c
columns.description: int, Sum of quantity of all Opportunity Products on this opportunity

columns.name: pricebook2id
columns.description: string, ID of the associated Pricebook2. Pricebook2 contains the standard or custom price book for product

columns.name: problems__c
columns.description: string, text field noted for problems

columns.name: topic__c
columns.description: topics for this opportunity

columns.name: business_impact__c
columns.description: string, text field noted for business impact

columns.name: createddate
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when this record was created by a user

columns.name: createdbyid
columns.description: string, ID of the User who created this opportunity

columns.name: lastmodifiedbyid
columns.description: string, ID of the User who last updated this opportunity

columns.name: lastmodifieddate
columns.description: timestamp, Date and time when this record was last updated by a user

columns.name: laststagechangedate
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the Stage was last changed

columns.name: systemmodstamp
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when a user or automated process last modified this record

columns.name: lastvieweddate
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the current user last viewed this record

columns.name: lastreferenceddate
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the current user last accessed this record

columns.name: lastamountchangedhistoryid
columns.description: string, ID of the OpportunityHistory record that contains information about when the opportunity Amount field was last updated

columns.name: lastclosedatechangedhistoryid
columns.description: string, ID of the OpportunityHistory record that contains information about when the closed date field was last updated

columns.name: lastactivitydate
columns.description: date, It's either the date of the most recent event or the most recently closed task associated with the opportunity record

columns.name: fiscalquarter
columns.description: int, fiscal quarter for the opportunity (auto-calculated by salesforce)

columns.name: fiscalyear
columns.description: int, fiscal year of the opportunity (auto-calculated by salesforce)

columns.name: fiscal
columns.description: string, combining fiscal year + fiscal quarter (auto-calculated by salesforce)

columns.name: contactid
columns.description: string, ID of the primary contact associated with this opportunity

columns.name: recordtypeid
columns.description: string, ID of the RecordType assigned to this opportunity

columns.name: campaignid
columns.description: string, ID of associated Campaign

columns.name: syncedquoteid
columns.description: string, ID of the Quote synced to the opportunity

columns.name: accountid
columns.description: string, account ID that the opportunity is linked to

columns.name: ownerid
columns.description: string, owner ID for this opportunity

columns.name: isdeleted
columns.description: boolean, indicating whether the record has been deleted

columns.name: _airbyte_ab_id
columns.description: string, uuid assigned to each row of the data by Airbyte connector

columns.name: _airbyte_emitted_at
columns.description: string, unix time string when the record was emitted and recorded by Airbyte connector. This field should be used internally to deduplicate records.

columns.name: id
columns.description: string, unique string that identifies this opportunity product line item

columns.name: name
columns.description: string, product name of the line item

columns.name: description
columns.description: string, text description of the line item

columns.name: productcode
columns.description: string, product code for the line item. e.g. program-v1:xPRO+SysEngx+R18

columns.name: listprice

columns.name: unitprice

columns.name: quantity

columns.name: totalprice

columns.name: opportunityid

columns.name: product2id
columns.description: string, ID of the associated Product2 (some are imported from xPro app), which represents a product

columns.name: pricebookentryid
columns.description: string, ID of the associated PricebookEntry (association between Pricebook2 and Product2). PricebookEntry defines the sales price for a product

columns.name: sortorder
columns.description: int, sort order selected by the user - currently all blank

columns.name: createddate
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when this record was created by a user

columns.name: createdbyid
columns.description: string, ID of the User who created this line item

columns.name: servicedate
columns.description: date, date when the product revenue will be recognized and the product quantity will be shipped

columns.name: lastmodifiedbyid
columns.description: string, ID of the User who last updated this line item

columns.name: lastmodifieddate
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when this line item was last updated by a user

columns.name: lastreferenceddate
columns.description: timestamp, date and time for when the current user last viewed this record

columns.name: lastvieweddate
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the current user last viewed this record

columns.name: systemmodstamp
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when a user or automated process last modified this line item

columns.name: isdeleted
columns.description: boolean, indicating whether the record has been deleted

columns.name: _airbyte_ab_id
columns.description: string, uuid assigned to each row of the data by Airbyte connector

columns.name: _airbyte_emitted_at
columns.description: string, unix time string when the record was emitted and recorded by Airbyte connector. This field should be used internally to deduplicate records.

columns.name: opportunity_id
columns.description: string, globally unique string that identifies an opportunity

columns.name: opportunity_name
columns.description: string, name for the opportunity

columns.name: opportunity_type
columns.description: string, opportunity type - Existing Business, Expansion Business, New Business

columns.name: opportunity_business_type
columns.description: string, indicating business type - B2C (Business to Consumer), B2B (Business to Business) or B2X (Business-to-Exchange). B2X is inactive. There are also other possible values e.g. 3rd Party – B2C

columns.name: opportunity_agreement_type
columns.description: string, type of Agreement. Possible values - 3rd Party Hosted, Additional MSA Order Form, Affiliate Direct, Affiliate via Intermediary, Bulk Sales, Content Licensing, Master Service Agreement, Membership, Private Course (SPOC), Reseller, Self-Pay Opp, Subscription, Training Broker, Underwriting, Workshop

columns.name: opportunity_revenue_share_or_commission_contract
columns.description: boolean, indicating if MIT will have to pay a fee or commission to another party because of this opportunity

columns.name: opportunity_revenue_share_or_commission_amount
columns.description: decimal, actual $ amount of revenue share/commission once it has been calculated for this opportunity

columns.name: opportunity_mit_is_sales_lead
columns.description: boolean, indicating if MIT is the lead (or unsupported sales) in opportunity for 3rd party hosted sales

columns.name: opportunity_stage
columns.description: string, current stage of the opportunity. Some examples - Closed Won, Closed Won, Enrollment List, Contracting, Demo, Order, Prospecting, Connecting, etc

columns.name: opportunity_leadsource
columns.description: string, source of the opportunity, such as Advertisement, Partner, or Web

columns.name: opportunity_nextstep
columns.description: string, next step noted for this opportunity

columns.name: opportunity_has_lineitem
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the opportunity has product line item

columns.name: opportunity_amount
columns.description: decimal, estimated total sale amount in US dollar

columns.name: opportunity_probability
columns.description: int, percentage of the likelihood that opportunity will close - 0 to 100 (0 means Closed/Lost, 100 means Closed/Won)

columns.name: opportunity_is_won
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the opportunity is a closed-Won sale

columns.name: opportunity_is_closed
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the opportunity is a closed sale, which can be Closed-Lost or Closed-Won

columns.name: opportunity_close_date
columns.description: date, close date for the opportunity

columns.name: opportunity_num_enrollment_codes_redeemed
columns.description: int, sum of enrollment codes redeemed

columns.name: opportunity_num_of_seats_purchased
columns.description: int, number of Seats purchased

columns.name: opportunity_price_per_seat
columns.description: int, price per seat, it's calculated Amount / of_Seats_Purchased__c in US dollar

columns.name: opportunity_data_quality_score
columns.description: int, data quality score between 0 to 100

columns.name: opportunity_data_quality_description
columns.description: string, description for data quality score

columns.description: timestamp, date and time when this record was created

columns.name: opportunity_modified_on

columns.name: website_uuid
columns.description: str, unique identifier for the OCW website

columns.name: website_name
columns.description: str, unique name of the website. It appears as part of OCW Studio urls

columns.name: website_short_id
columns.description: str, short unique identifier for the OCW website. e.g., 18.05-spring-2022

columns.name: website_title
columns.description: str, title of the OCW website

columns.name: website_owner_user_id
columns.description: int, foreign key to users_user, indicating who created the OCW website

columns.name: websitestarter_id
columns.description: int, foreign key to websites_websitestarter, indicating a starter project that contains config/templates/etc for the desired static site. The OCW courses reference to ocw-course in websites_websitestarter.

columns.name: website_source
columns.description: str, indicating which sites were imported and which ones were authored in the app. Possible values are 'studio', 'ocw-import', or blank

columns.name: website_url_path
columns.description: str, unique url relative path to the OCW website. e.g., courses/my-site-fall-2020

columns.name: website_live_url
columns.description: str, webiste url on OCW production.

columns.name: website_is_live
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the website is currently published on OCW production.

columns.name: website_is_unpublished
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the website is currently unpublished on OCW production.

columns.name: website_has_never_published
columns.description: boolean, indicating if the website has never been published on OCW production.

columns.name: website_metadata
columns.description: json, the json object that contains website's metadata such as description, course number, topics, department_numbers, instructors, etc. Full list of metadata fields can be found at https://github.com/mitodl/ocw-hugo-projects/blob/main/ocw-course-v2/ocw-studio.yaml

columns.name: website_last_published_by_user_id
columns.description: int, foreign key to users_user, indicating who most recently published the website to OCW production

columns.name: website_last_unpublished_by_user_id
columns.description: int, foreign key to users_user, indicating who most recently unpublished the website on OCW production

columns.name: website_publish_status
columns.description: str, indicating the website's publish status on OCW production. Possible values are succeeded, pending, started, errored, aborted and not-started

columns.name: website_unpublish_status
columns.description: str, indicating the status when website is unpublished on OCW production. Possible values are succeeded, pending, started, errored, aborted and not-started

columns.name: website_first_published_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the website was first published to OCW production

columns.name: website_publish_date_updated_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the publish date was most recently updated on OCW production. The publish date is updated by the webhook to Concourse pipeline (it rebuilds every site) whenever we do a release in ocw repos.

columns.name: website_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the website record was created

columns.name: website_updated_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when the website record was last updated

columns.name: primary_course_number
columns.description: str, primary course number of the course

columns.name: metadata_course_term
columns.description: str, course term extracted from metadata

columns.name: metadata_course_title
columns.description: str, course title extracted from metadata

columns.name: metadata_course_year
columns.description: str, course year extracted from metadata

schema_snapshot.description: OCW websites metadata on production environment. A website can refers to OCW courses, OCW website configuration, etc.

columns.name: websitecontent_id
columns.description: int, primary key for this OCW website content component

columns.name: website_uuid
columns.description: str, foreign key to websites_website

columns.name: websitecontent_text_id
columns.description: str, uuid string for the page content or sitemetadata

columns.name: websitecontent_title
columns.description: str, title of this content component

columns.name: websitecontent_type
columns.description: str, type of this content component. e.g., page, sitemetadata, resource, etc.

columns.name: websitecontent_is_page
columns.description: boolean, indicating that this content represents a navigable page, as opposed to site metadata, configuration, etc.

columns.name: websitecontent_metadata
columns.description: json, the json object that contains the metadata of this content component

columns.name: websitecontent_filename
columns.description: str, filename of the page content without the file extension.

columns.name: websitecontent_dirpath
columns.description: str, the directory path for the page content. e.g., content/resources

columns.name: websitecontent_file
columns.description: str, url path for the page content.

columns.name: websitecontent_parent_id
columns.description: int, foreign key to websites_websitecontent, indicating the parent of this content

columns.name: websitecontent_owner_user_id
columns.description: int, foreign key to users_user, indicating who created this content component

columns.name: websitecontent_updated_by_user_id
columns.description: int, foreign key to users_user, indicating who last updated this content component

columns.name: course_description
columns.description: str, course description extracted from sitemetadata

columns.name: course_term
columns.description: str, course term extracted from sitemetadata. Possible values are Spring, Summer, Fall, January IAP, or blank

columns.name: course_year
columns.description: int, course year extracted from sitemetadata.

columns.name: course_level
columns.description: str, course level extracted from sitemetadata. Possible values are Undergraduate, Graduate, Non-Credit, High School, or blank

columns.name: course_title
columns.description: str, course title extracted from sitemetadata

columns.name: course_topics
columns.description: json, hierarchical course topics (topic, subtopic, speciality) from sitemetadata. e.g., [["Mathematics","Discrete Mathematics"],["Mathematics","Probability and Statistics"]]

columns.name: course_instructor_uuids
columns.description: json, course instructor's uuid extracted from sitemetadata

columns.name: learning_resource_types
columns.description: str, learning resource types extracted from metadata e.g., Lecture Notes, Problem Sets with Solutions...

columns.name: course_department_numbers
columns.description: str, course department numbers in comma-separated list. e.g., STS, 8..

columns.name: course_department_numbers_json
columns.description: json, course department numbers in json array. e.g., ["STS", "8"..]

columns.name: course_primary_course_number
columns.description: str, the primary course number extracted from sitemetadata. e.g., 21A.850J

columns.name: course_extra_course_numbers
columns.description: str, the extra course number extracted from sitemetadata. e.g., STS.484J

columns.name: websitecontent_deleted_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time for the soft delete

columns.name: websitecontent_created_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when this website content component was created

columns.name: websitecontent_updated_on
columns.description: timestamp, date and time when this website content component was last updated

columns.name: metadata_body
columns.description: str, body of the metadata

columns.name: metadata_description
columns.description: str, description of the metadata

columns.name: metadata_draft
columns.description: boolean, resource draft status, according to metadata

columns.name: metadata_file
columns.description: str, file associated with the resource

columns.name: metadata_file_size
columns.description: int, size of the file associated with the resource

columns.name: metadata_license
columns.description: str, license of the image or external resource

columns.name: metadata_legacy_type
columns.description: str, legacy type (from Plone)

columns.name: metadata_resource_type
columns.description: str, resource type, according to metadata

columns.name: metadata_title
columns.description: str, title, according to metadata

columns.name: metadata_uid
columns.description: str, UUID of the resource, according to metadata

schema_snapshot.description: OCW website content components such as page, resource, sitemetadata, etc.

columns.description: str, short string to identify this starter. e.g., ocw-course-v2
